content.text:
Here is a complete summary of the findings:

---

**1. OpenTelemetry deps in `server/mix.exs`**

The following opentelemetry-related deps are declared (lines 146-155):

```elixir
{:opentelemetry_api, "~> 1.4"},
{:opentelemetry, "~> 1.5"},
{:opentelemetry_exporter, "~> 1.8"},
{:opentelemetry_phoenix, "~> 2.0"},
{:opentelemetry_ecto, github: "open-telemetry/opentelemetry-erlang-contrib", sparse: "instrumentation/opentelemetry_ecto"},
{:opentelemetry_finch, "~> 0.2"},
{:opentelemetry_logger_metadata, "~> 0.1"},
{:opentelemetry_bandit, "~> 0.3"},
{:opentelemetry_broadway, "~> 0.3"},
```

**2. `:opentelemetry_exporter` is a dep**

Yes, it is present and locked at version `1.10.0` (resolved from `~> 1.8`).

**3. The protobuf decoder is available**

The file at `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/deps/opentelemetry_exporter/src/opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb.erl` exists and exports:

```erlang
-export([decode_msg/2, decode_msg/3]).
```

It supports decoding `export_trace_service_request` directly (line 1103):

```erlang
decode_msg_2_doit(export_trace_service_request, Bin, TrUserData) ->
    id(decode_msg_export_trace_service_request(Bin, TrUserData), TrUserData);
```

The Erlang module name is `:opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb`. You can call it from Elixir as:

```elixir
:opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb.decode_msg(binary, :export_trace_service_request)
```

**4. No existing usage in the codebase**

There are no existing references to `:opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb`, `decode_msg` for trace service, or `ExportTraceServiceRequest` anywhere in the `server/` source. This module is brought in transitively via `:opentelemetry_exporter` but has not yet been used directly in application code.

**Conclusion:** The protobuf decoder for OTLP traces is fully available through `:opentelemetry_exporter` 1.10.0 (declared as `~> 1.8`). The module `:opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb` is compiled as part of that dep and exposes `decode_msg/2` and `decode_msg/3`, accepting the atom `:export_trace_service_request` as the message name.

    84→      Tuist.AppBuilds.latest_previews_with_distinct_bundle_ids(project)
    85→    )
    86→  end
    87→
    88→  defp assign_test_runs_analytics(%{assigns: %{selected_project: project}} = socket) do
    89→    {recent_test_runs, _meta} =
    90→      Tests.list_test_runs(%{
    91→        last: 40,
    92→        filters: [
    93→          %{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project.id}
    94→        ],
    95→        order_by: [:ran_at],
    96→        order_directions: [:asc]
    97→      })
    98→
    99→    recent_test_runs_chart_data =
   100→      Enum.map(recent_test_runs, fn run ->
   101→        color =
   102→          case run.status do
   103→            "success" -> "var:noora-chart-primary"
   104→            "failure" -> "var:noora-chart-destructive"
   105→            "skipped" -> "var:noora-chart-warning"
   106→          end
   107→
   108→        value = (run.duration / 1000) |> Decimal.from_float() |> Decimal.round(0)
   109→
   110→        %{value: value, itemStyle: %{color: color}, date: run.ran_at}
   111→      end)
   112→
   113→    failed_test_runs_count = Enum.count(recent_test_runs, fn run -> run.status == "failure" end)
   114→
   115→    passed_test_runs_count =
   116→      Enum.count(recent_test_runs, fn run -> run.status == "success" end)
   117→
   118→    socket
   119→    |> assign(:recent_test_runs, recent_test_runs_chart_data)
   120→    |> assign(:failed_test_runs_count, failed_test_runs_count)
   121→    |> assign(:passed_test_runs_count, passed_test_runs_count)
   122→  end

   127→

   129→      DatePicker.date_picker_params(params, "bundle-size")
   130→

   132→

   134→      project
   135→      |> Bundles.project_bundle_install_size_analytics(opts)
   136→      |> Enum.map(
   137→        &[
   138→          &1.date,
   139→          &1.bundle_install_size
   140→        ]
   141→      )
   142→
   143→    socket
   144→    |> assign(:bundle_size_selected_app, bundle_size_selected_app)
   145→    |> assign(:bundle_size_apps, Enum.map(bundle_size_apps, & &1.name))
   146→    |> assign(:bundle_size_preset, preset)
   147→    |> assign(:bundle_size_period, period)
   148→    |> assign(:bundle_size_analytics, bundle_size_analytics)
   149→  end

1→defmodule Noora.Chart do 2→ @moduledoc """ 3→ A powerful charting component powered by ECharts. 4→ 5→ ## Example 6→ 7→ ```elixir 8→ <.chart 9→ id="revenue-chart" 10→ type="line" 11→ series={[10, 20, 30, 40]} 12→ labels={["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"]} 13→ title="Quarterly Revenue" 14→ /> 15→ ``` 16→ 17→ ## Custom formatters 18→ 19→ For labels, we offer several built-in formatters: 20→ 21→ - `formatSeconds`: Formats seconds into human readable time (e.g., "30s", "2m 30s", "1h 5m") 22→ - `formatMilliseconds`: Formats milliseconds into human readable time 23→ - `formatHours`: Formats hours into human readable time (e.g., "30m", "2h 30m", "1d 5h") 24→ - `formatBytes`: Formats bytes into human readable size (e.g., "1.5 KB", "2 MB", "1 GB") 25→ - `toLocaleDate`: Formats dates into locale-specific format 26→ - `firstAndLastDate`: Renders the first and last label only as dates. This is useful for time series charts. 27→ 28→ If this does not match your use case, you can also pass a completely custom formatter. This expects a global `nooraChartFormatters` object 29→ with a function named after the formatter you want to use. For example, if you want to use a "first label only" formatter, you can define 30→ a function named `firstLabelOnly` in the `nooraChartFormatters` object as such: 31→ 32→ ```js 33→ nooraChartFormatters = { 34→ firstLabelOnly: (el) => (value, index) => { 35→ if (index === 0) { 36→ return value; 37→ } 38→ } 39→ }; 40→ ``` 41→ 42→ You can use these function-based formatters in your chart configuration as such: 43→ 44→ ```elixir 45→ %{ 46→ extra_options: %{ 47→ xAxis: %{ 48→ axisLabel: %{ 49→ formatter: "fn:firstLabelOnly" 50→ } 51→ } 52→ } 53→ ``` 54→ """ 55→ use Phoenix.Component 56→ 57→ attr(:id, :string, required: true, doc: "The ID used for the chart container") 58→ 59→ attr(:type, :string, 60→ default: "bar", 61→ values: ["bar", "line", "pie", "scatter", "radar"], 62→ doc: """ 63→ The type of chart to render. Defaults to "bar". 64→ Available types: bar, line, pie, scatter, radar 65→ """ 66→ ) 67→ 68→ attr(:series, :any, 69→ default: nil, 70→ doc: """ 71→ The series data for the chart. Structure depends on chart type: 72→ 73→ For bar/line charts: 74→ - A list of values: [10, 20, 30] 75→ - A list of maps with names: [%{name: "Series 1", values: [10, 20, 30]}] 76→ - A list of series with different types: [%{name: "Bar", type: "bar", values: [10, 20, 30]}, %{name: "Line", type: "line", values: [5, 15, 25]}] 77→ - A list of time series: [%{name: "Series 1", data: [["2024-01-01", 10], ["2024-01-02", 20]]}] 78→ 79→ For pie charts: 80→ - A list of maps with name and value: [%{name: "A", value: 10}, %{name: "B", value: 20}] 81→ 82→ For scatter charts: 83→ - A list of [x, y] points: [[10, 20], [30, 40]] 84→ - A list of maps with series: [%{name: "Series 1", data: [[10, 20], [30, 40]]}] 85→ """ 86→ ) 87→ 88→ attr(:labels, :list, 89→ default: [], 90→ doc: """ 91→ Category labels for the x-axis (for bar/line charts) or data point names (for pie charts). 92→ Example: ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"] 93→ """ 94→ ) 95→ 96→ attr(:title, :string, 97→ default: nil, 98→ doc: """ 99→ Main title for the chart. 100→ """ 101→ ) 102→ 103→ attr(:subtitle, :string, 104→ default: nil, 105→ doc: """ 106→ Subtitle shown below the main title. 107→ """ 108→ ) 109→ 110→ attr(:show_legend, :boolean, 111→ default: true, 112→ doc: """ 113→ Whether to show the chart legend. 114→ """ 115→ ) 116→ 117→ attr(:legend_position, :string, 118→ default: "top", 119→ values: ["top", "bottom", "left", "right"], 120→ doc: """ 121→ Position of the legend. Options: "top", "bottom", "left", "right" 122→ """ 123→ ) 124→ 125→ attr(:colors, :list, 126→ default: [ 127→ "var:noora-chart-primary", 128→ "var:noora-chart-secondary", 129→ "var:noora-chart-tertiary", 130→ "var:noora-chart-quaternary" 131→ ], 132→ doc: """ 133→ Custom colors for data series. 134→ 135→ The given items can either be a hex string, such as "#4C9AFF", or a CSS variable, prefixed with `var:`, but omitting the `--` prefix, 136→ such as `chart-primary`. 137→ The CSS variable is expected to be on the `:root` element, so the variable would be defined as such: 138→ 139→ ```css 140→ :root { 141→ --chart-primary: #4C9AFF; 142→ } 143→ ``` 144→ 145→ In addition, the color setting supports the `light-dark()` function, which can be used to automatically resolve the appropriate color 146→ based on the the color scheme. That way, the variable can be defined as such: 147→ 148→ ```css 149→ :root { 150→ --chart-primary: light-dark(#4C9AFF, #36B37E); 151→ } 152→ ``` 153→ 154→ Example: ["#4C9AFF", "#36B37E", "#FF5630", "#FFAB00", "#6554C0"] 155→ """ 156→ ) 157→ 158→ attr(:stacked, :boolean, 159→ default: false, 160→ doc: """ 161→ For bar/line charts, whether series should be stacked. 162→ """ 163→ ) 164→ 165→ attr(:show_values, :boolean, 166→ default: false, 167→ doc: """ 168→ Whether to show data values directly on the chart. 169→ """ 170→ ) 171→ 172→ attr(:smooth, :boolean, 173→ default: false, 174→ doc: """ 175→ For line charts, whether to use smooth curves instead of straight lines. 176→ """ 177→ ) 178→ 179→ attr(:donut, :boolean, 180→ default: false, 181→ doc: """ 182→ For pie charts, whether to render as a donut chart. 183→ """ 184→ ) 185→ 186→ attr(:donut_radius, :list, 187→ default: ["50%", "70%"], 188→ doc: """ 189→ For donut charts, the inner and outer radius. Default: ["50%", "70%"] 190→ """ 191→ ) 192→ 193→ attr(:x_axis_name, :string, 194→ default: nil, 195→ doc: """ 196→ Name for the X axis. Example: "Month" 197→ """ 198→ ) 199→ 200→ attr(:y_axis_name, :string, 201→ default: nil, 202→ doc: """ 203→ Name for the Y axis. Example: "Revenue ($)" 204→ """ 205→ ) 206→ 207→ attr(:x_axis_min, :integer, 208→ default: nil, 209→ doc: """ 210→ Minimum value for the X axis. Example: 0 211→ """ 212→ ) 213→ 214→ attr(:x_axis_max, :integer, 215→ default: nil, 216→ doc: """ 217→ Maximum value for the X axis. Example: 100 218→ """ 219→ ) 220→ 221→ attr(:y_axis_min, :integer, 222→ default: nil, 223→ doc: """ 224→ Minimum value for the Y axis. Example: 0 225→ """ 226→ ) 227→ 228→ attr(:y_axis_max, :integer, 229→ default: nil, 230→ doc: """ 231→ Maximum value for the Y axis. Example: 100 232→ """ 233→ ) 234→ 235→ attr(:grid_lines, :boolean, 236→ default: true, 237→ doc: """ 238→ Whether to show grid lines on the chart. 239→ """ 240→ ) 241→ 242→ attr(:horizontal, :boolean, 243→ default: false, 244→ doc: """ 245→ Whether to display a horizontal bar chart (with category axis on y-axis). 246→ """ 247→ ) 248→ 249→ attr(:bar_width, :integer, 250→ default: nil, 251→ doc: """ 252→ Width of bars in bar charts (in pixels). 253→ """ 254→ ) 255→ 256→ attr(:bar_radius, :integer, 257→ default: nil, 258→ doc: """ 259→ Border radius of bars in bar charts (in pixels). 260→ """ 261→ ) 262→ 263→ attr(:extra_options, :map, 264→ default: %{}, 265→ doc: """ 266→ Additional ECharts options for advanced customization. 267→ These will be deeply merged with options generated from other attributes. 268→ """ 269→ ) 270→ 271→ attr(:rest, :global, doc: "Additional HTML attributes to add to the container div") 272→ 273→ def chart(assigns) do 274→ # Load chart type specific defaults and build the complete chart options 275→ type_defaults = get_type_defaults(assigns.type) 276→ chart_options = build_options(assigns, type_defaults) 277→ assigns = assign(assigns, option: chart_options) 278→ 279→ ~H""" 280→
281→ {# The actual chart is managed by ECharts, so we are ignoring any updates by LiveView. The chart should be updated by changing the component attributes. #} 282→
283→
{Jason.encode!(@option)}
284→
285→ """ 286→ end 287→ 288→ # Main function to build all chart options by combining defaults with user-provided configuration 289→ defp build_options(assigns, type_defaults) do 290→ # Start with default options and merge with chart type-specific defaults 291→ base_options = DeepMerge.deep_merge(default(), type_defaults) 292→ 293→ # Extract specific attributes to avoid passing full assigns map to helper functions 294→ title = assigns.title 295→ subtitle = assigns.subtitle 296→ chart_type = assigns.type 297→ show_legend = assigns.show_legend 298→ legend_position = assigns.legend_position 299→ colors = assigns.colors 300→ grid_lines = assigns.grid_lines 301→ horizontal = assigns.horizontal 302→ labels = assigns.labels 303→ 304→ # Axis configuration 305→ axis_config = %{ 306→ x_axis_name: assigns.x_axis_name, 307→ y_axis_name: assigns.y_axis_name, 308→ x_axis_min: assigns.x_axis_min, 309→ x_axis_max: assigns.x_axis_max, 310→ y_axis_min: assigns.y_axis_min, 311→ y_axis_max: assigns.y_axis_max, 312→ extra_options: assigns.extra_options, 313→ horizontal: horizontal, 314→ labels: labels 315→ } 316→ 317→ # Series configuration 318→ series_config = %{ 319→ stacked: assigns.stacked, 320→ show_values: assigns.show_values, 321→ smooth: assigns.smooth, 322→ donut: assigns.donut, 323→ donut_radius: assigns.donut_radius, 324→ bar_width: assigns.bar_width, 325→ bar_radius: assigns.bar_radius, 326→ horizontal: horizontal 327→ } 328→ 329→ # Build options by adding each component 330→ custom_options = 331→ %{} 332→ |> add_title_options(title, subtitle) 333→ |> add_legend_options(show_legend, legend_position) 334→ |> add_tooltip_options(chart_type) 335→ |> add_colors(colors) 336→ |> add_grid_options(grid_lines) 337→ |> add_axis_options(chart_type, axis_config) 338→ |> add_series_options(chart_type, assigns.series, labels, series_config) 339→ 340→ # Merge the base options with custom options and extra options 341→ merged_options = 342→ base_options 343→ |> DeepMerge.deep_merge(custom_options) 344→ |> DeepMerge.deep_merge(Map.delete(assigns.extra_options, :series)) 345→ 346→ # Apply bar radius to all bar series if it's set 347→ merged_options = 348→ if chart_type == "bar" && assigns.bar_radius do 349→ apply_bar_radius_to_series(merged_options, assigns.bar_radius) 350→ else 351→ merged_options 352→ end 353→ 354→ merged_options 355→ end 356→ 357→ # Adds chart title and subtitle if provided 358→ defp add_title_options(options, title, subtitle) do 359→ title_opts = 360→ %{} 361→ |> maybe_add_to_map(:text, title) 362→ |> maybe_add_to_map(:subtext, subtitle) 363→ 364→ if map_size(title_opts) > 0 do 365→ Map.put(options, :title, title_opts) 366→ else 367→ options 368→ end 369→ end 370→ 371→ # Configures the legend display and position 372→ defp add_legend_options(options, show_legend, legend_position) do 373→ if show_legend do 374→ legend_position = 375→ case legend_position do 376→ "top" -> %{top: "0", left: "center"} 377→ "bottom" -> %{bottom: "0", left: "center"} 378→ "left" -> %{left: "0", top: "center", orient: "vertical"} 379→ "right" -> %{right: "0", top: "center", orient: "vertical"} 380→ _ -> %{top: "0", left: "center"} 381→ end 382→ 383→ Map.put(options, :legend, legend_position) 384→ else 385→ Map.put(options, :legend, %{show: false}) 386→ end 387→ end 388→ 389→ # Sets tooltip trigger based on chart type (item for pie, axis for others) 390→ defp add_tooltip_options(options, chart_type) do 391→ trigger_value = if chart_type == "pie", do: "item", else: "axis" 392→ 393→ tooltip = %{ 394→ trigger: trigger_value 395→ } 396→ 397→ Map.put(options, :tooltip, tooltip) 398→ end 399→ 400→ # Adds custom color configuration if provided 401→ defp add_colors(options, colors) do 402→ if length(colors) > 0 do 403→ Map.put(options, :colors, colors) 404→ else 405→ options 406→ end 407→ end 408→ 409→ # Configures grid display 410→ defp add_grid_options(options, grid_lines) do 411→ grid = Map.get(options, :grid, %{}) 412→ 413→ grid = 414→ if grid_lines do 415→ grid 416→ else 417→ Map.put(grid, :show, false) 418→ end 419→ 420→ Map.put(options, :grid, grid) 421→ end 422→ 423→ # Handles axis configuration based on chart type 424→ defp add_axis_options(options, chart_type, axis_config) do 425→ # Skip axis configuration for pie and radar charts 426→ if chart_type in ["pie", "radar"] do 427→ options 428→ else 429→ x_axis = build_axis_options(:x, axis_config) 430→ y_axis = build_axis_options(:y, axis_config) 431→ 432→ # Handle horizontal bar charts by swapping axis types 433→ {x_axis, y_axis} = 434→ if axis_config.horizontal && chart_type == "bar" do 435→ # For horizontal bars, x is value axis, y is category axis 436→ x_axis = Map.put_new(x_axis, :type, "value") 437→ y_axis = Map.put_new(y_axis, :type, "category") 438→ 439→ # Add labels to y-axis for horizontal charts 440→ # credo:disable-for-next-line 441→ if length(axis_config.labels) > 0 do 442→ y_axis = Map.put(y_axis, :data, axis_config.labels) 443→ x_axis = Map.delete(x_axis, :data) 444→ {x_axis, y_axis} 445→ else 446→ {x_axis, y_axis} 447→ end 448→ else 449→ # Default orientation: x is category, y is value 450→ x_axis = Map.put_new(x_axis, :type, "category") 451→ y_axis = Map.put_new(y_axis, :type, "value") 452→ {x_axis, y_axis} 453→ end 454→ 455→ options 456→ |> Map.put(:xAxis, x_axis) 457→ |> Map.put(:yAxis, y_axis) 458→ end 459→ end 460→ 461→ # Builds the axis configuration for either x or y axis 462→ # credo:disable-for-next-line 463→ defp build_axis_options(axis_type, config) do 464→ axis_key = if axis_type == :x, do: :xAxis, else: :yAxis 465→ # Start with any user-provided axis config from extra_options 466→ base_axis = Map.get(config.extra_options, axis_key, %{}) 467→ 468→ # Add axis name if provided 469→ axis_name = if axis_type == :x, do: config.x_axis_name, else: config.y_axis_name 470→ base_axis = if axis_name, do: Map.put(base_axis, :name, axis_name), else: base_axis 471→ 472→ # Add min/max values if provided 473→ min_value = if axis_type == :x, do: config.x_axis_min, else: config.y_axis_min 474→ max_value = if axis_type == :x, do: config.x_axis_max, else: config.y_axis_max 475→ 476→ base_axis = if min_value, do: Map.put(base_axis, :min, min_value), else: base_axis 477→ base_axis = if max_value, do: Map.put(base_axis, :max, max_value), else: base_axis 478→ 479→ # Add labels as data for the x-axis (when not horizontal) 480→ if axis_type == :x && length(config.labels) > 0 && !config.horizontal do 481→ Map.put(base_axis, :data, config.labels) 482→ else 483→ base_axis 484→ end 485→ end 486→ 487→ # Configures series data based on chart type and provided data 488→ defp add_series_options(options, _chart_type, nil, _labels, _config) do 489→ # No series data provided 490→ options 491→ end 492→ 493→ defp add_series_options(options, chart_type, series, labels, config) do 494→ processed_series = process_data_for_chart_type(chart_type, series, labels, config) 495→ Map.put(options, :series, processed_series) 496→ end 497→ 498→ # Process data for different chart types 499→ defp process_data_for_chart_type(type, series, labels, opts) when type in ["bar", "line"] do 500→ process_bar_line_data(type, series, labels, opts) 501→ end 502→ 503→ # Pie chart configuration 504→ defp process_data_for_chart_type("pie", series, _labels, opts) do 505→ [ 506→ %{ 507→ type: "pie", 508→ radius: if(opts.donut, do: opts.donut_radius, else: "60%"), 509→ data: normalize_pie_data(series), 510→ label: %{ 511→ show: opts.show_values 512→ } 513→ } 514→ ] 515→ end 516→ 517→ # Scatter chart configuration 518→ defp process_data_for_chart_type("scatter", series, _labels, opts) do 519→ normalize_scatter_data(series, opts) 520→ end 521→ 522→ # Radar chart configuration 523→ defp process_data_for_chart_type("radar", series, _labels, _opts) do 524→ [%{type: "radar", data: normalize_radar_data(series)}] 525→ end 526→ 527→ # Default fallback for other chart types 528→ defp process_data_for_chart_type(_type, series, _labels, _opts) do 529→ # Default fallback - pass through the data for advanced usage 530→ series 531→ end 532→ 533→ # Processes data for bar and line charts, handling different input formats 534→ # credo:disable-for-next-line 535→ defp process_bar_line_data(type, series, _labels, opts) do 536→ cond do 537→ # Simple array of numbers: [10, 20, 30] 538→ is_list(series) && series |> List.first() |> is_number() -> 539→ [ 540→ build_series_config(%{ 541→ type: type, 542→ data: series, 543→ opts: opts 544→ }) 545→ ] 546→ 547→ # Array of series objects: [%{name: "Series 1", values: [10, 20, 30]}] 548→ is_list(series) && series |> List.first() |> is_map() -> 549→ Enum.map(series, fn series_item -> 550→ # Support both 'values' and 'data' keys for consistency 551→ values = series_item[:values] || series_item[:data] || [] 552→ 553→ # Extract known properties we handle 554→ known_props = [:type, :name, :values, :data, :show_values, :smooth] 555→ 556→ # Get all additional series options that should be passed through 557→ additional_options = Map.drop(series_item, known_props) 558→ 559→ build_series_config(%{ 560→ # Allow mixed chart types (bar/line) 561→ type: series_item[:type] || type, 562→ name: series_item[:name] || "", 563→ data: values, 564→ # Override global setting per series 565→ show_values: series_item[:show_values], 566→ # Override global setting per series 567→ smooth: series_item[:smooth], 568→ additional_options: additional_options, 569→ opts: opts 570→ }) 571→ end) 572→ 573→ # Fallback for any other data format 574→ true -> 575→ [ 576→ build_series_config(%{ 577→ type: type, 578→ data: series, 579→ opts: opts 580→ }) 581→ ] 582→ end 583→ end 584→ 585→ # Creates configuration for a single chart series 586→ defp build_series_config(%{type: type, data: data, opts: opts} = config) do 587→ # Base series configuration 588→ series = %{ 589→ type: type, 590→ data: data, 591→ stack: if(opts.stacked, do: "total"), 592→ label: %{ 593→ show: config[:show_values] || opts.show_values 594→ } 595→ } 596→ 597→ # Add line chart specific options 598→ series = 599→ if type == "line" do 600→ series 601→ |> Map.put(:smooth, config[:smooth] || opts.smooth) 602→ # Remove data point dots 603→ |> Map.put(:symbol, "none") 604→ else 605→ series 606→ end 607→ 608→ # Add bar chart specific options 609→ series = 610→ if type == "bar" do 611→ series 612→ |> maybe_add_to_map(:barWidth, opts.bar_width) 613→ |> maybe_add_item_style(opts.bar_radius) 614→ else 615→ series 616→ end 617→ 618→ # Add series name if provided 619→ series = if config[:name], do: Map.put(series, :name, config[:name]), else: series 620→ 621→ # Merge any additional options passed directly from the series item 622→ if config[:additional_options] && map_size(config[:additional_options]) > 0 do 623→ Map.merge(series, config[:additional_options]) 624→ else 625→ series 626→ end 627→ end 628→ 629→ # Helper to add item style with border radius to bar charts 630→ defp maybe_add_item_style(series, nil), do: series 631→ 632→ defp maybe_add_item_style(series, bar_radius) do 633→ item_style = Map.get(series, :itemStyle, %{}) 634→ item_style = Map.put(item_style, :borderRadius, bar_radius) 635→ Map.put(series, :itemStyle, item_style) 636→ end 637→ 638→ # Apply border radius to all bar series in a chart 639→ defp apply_bar_radius_to_series(options, bar_radius) do 640→ case Map.get(options, :series) do 641→ nil -> 642→ options 643→ 644→ series when is_list(series) -> 645→ updated_series = 646→ Enum.map(series, fn series_item -> 647→ # credo:disable-for-next-line 648→ if series_item[:type] == "bar" do 649→ item_style = Map.get(series_item, :itemStyle, %{}) 650→ item_style = Map.put(item_style, :borderRadius, bar_radius) 651→ Map.put(series_item, :itemStyle, item_style) 652→ else 653→ series_item 654→ end 655→ end) 656→ 657→ Map.put(options, :series, updated_series) 658→ 659→ _ -> 660→ options 661→ end 662→ end 663→ 664→ # Normalizes pie chart data to expected format 665→ # credo:disable-for-next-line 666→ defp normalize_pie_data(series) when is_list(series) do 667→ cond do 668→ # Series already in correct format: [%{name: "A", value: 10}] 669→ series |> List.first() |> is_map() && Map.has_key?(List.first(series), :value) -> 670→ series 671→ 672→ # Series in different format: [%{name: "A", count: 10}] 673→ series |> List.first() |> is_map() -> 674→ Enum.map(series, fn item -> 675→ %{ 676→ name: item[:name] || item[:label] || "", 677→ value: item[:value] || item[:count] || 0 678→ } 679→ end) 680→ 681→ # Just values without names: [10, 20, 30] 682→ series |> List.first() |> is_number() -> 683→ Enum.with_index(series, fn value, index -> 684→ %{name: "Item #{index + 1}", value: value} 685→ end) 686→ 687→ # Fallback for any other format 688→ true -> 689→ series 690→ end 691→ end 692→ 693→ # Normalizes scatter chart data to expected format 694→ defp normalize_scatter_data(series, _opts) when is_list(series) do 695→ cond do 696→ # Multiple series: [%{name: "Series 1", data: [[10, 20], [30, 40]]}] 697→ series |> List.first() |> is_map() && 698→ (Map.has_key?(List.first(series), :data) || Map.has_key?(List.first(series), :values)) -> 699→ Enum.map(series, fn series_item -> 700→ values = series_item[:data] || series_item[:values] || [] 701→ 702→ %{ 703→ type: "scatter", 704→ name: series_item[:name] || "", 705→ data: values 706→ } 707→ end) 708→ 709→ # Direct array of points: [[10, 20], [30, 40]] 710→ series |> List.first() |> is_list() -> 711→ [ 712→ %{ 713→ type: "scatter", 714→ data: series 715→ } 716→ ] 717→ 718→ # Fallback for any other format 719→ true -> 720→ [%{type: "scatter", data: series}] 721→ end 722→ end 723→ 724→ # Hide axis line and ticks by default 755→ axisLine: %{show: false}, 756→ axisTick: %{show: false} 757→ }, 758→ yAxis: %{ 759→ axisLabel: %{fontSize: 10}, 760→ splitLine: %{ 761→ lineStyle: %{ 762→ type: "dashed" 763→ } 764→ }, 765→ # Hide axis line and ticks by default 766→ axisLine: %{show: false}, 767→ axisTick: %{show: false} 768→ }, 769→ textStyle: %{ 770→ fontFamily: "Geist Mono" 771→ } 772→ } 773→ end 774→ 775→ # Chart type specific default configurations 776→ defp get_type_defaults("scatter") do 777→ %{ 778→ xAxis: %{ 779→ axisLabel: %{fontSize: 10}, 780→ # Scale axes to data range rather than starting at zero 781→ scale: true 782→ }, 783→ yAxis: %{ 784→ axisLabel: %{fontSize: 10}, 785→ scale: true 786→ } 787→ } 788→ end 789→ 790→ defp get_type_defaults("radar") do 791→ %{ 792→ radar: %{ 793→ shape: "circle", 794→ # Will be populated with data categories 795→ indicator: [] 796→ } 797→ } 798→ end 799→ 800→ defp get_type_defaults(_) do 801→ %{} 802→ end 803→end 804→

data.prompt:
Search the server codebase for how the Noora `.chart` component is used in LiveView templates. I need to understand:

1. Search for `.chart` in heex files under server/lib/tuist_web/
2. Look at how chart data is structured (the assigns passed to the chart component)
3. Find any ECharts-related JavaScript code in server/assets/
4. Also search for `Noora.chart` or `noora_chart` or similar patterns

Return exact code examples showing how charts are rendered in templates and how data is passed to them.

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is a thorough summary of everything found: --- ## How `<.chart>` is Used in LiveView Templates ### 1. Component Source The `.chart` component is defined in the Noora design system package at: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/deps/noora/lib/noora/chart.ex` It is powered by **ECharts** under the hood. The component is brought into each LiveView module via `use Noora` (e.g., `use Noora` at the top of `xcode_overview_live.ex`). The JavaScript hook `NooraChart` is registered via `Noora.Hooks` in `app.js`: ```js // server/assets/app/app.js import Noora from "noora"; let liveSocket = new LiveSocket("/live", Socket, { hooks: { ...Hooks, ...Noora.Hooks }, }); ``` The rendered HTML structure is: ```html
{Jason.encode!(@option)}
``` ECharts reads the JSON-encoded options from the hidden `data-part="data"` div. --- ### 2. Full Component Attribute API From `chart.ex`, all supported attributes: | Attribute | Type | Default | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `id` | string | required | Container element ID | | `type` | string | `"bar"` | Chart type: `"bar"`, `"line"`, `"pie"`, `"scatter"`, `"radar"` | | `series` | any | nil | Data series (see formats below) | | `labels` | list | `[]` | X-axis category labels | | `title` | string | nil | Chart title | | `subtitle` | string | nil | Chart subtitle | | `show_legend` | boolean | true | Show/hide legend | | `legend_position` | string | `"top"` | `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`, `"right"` | | `colors` | list | Noora defaults | Series colors as hex or `"var:css-var-name"` | | `stacked` | boolean | false | Stack series | | `show_values` | boolean | false | Show value labels on chart | | `smooth` | boolean | false | Smooth line curves | | `donut` | boolean | false | Donut style for pie charts | | `donut_radius` | list | `["50%", "70%"]` | Inner/outer donut radius | | `x_axis_name` | string | nil | X axis label | | `y_axis_name` | string | nil | Y axis label | | `x_axis_min` | integer | nil | X axis minimum | | `x_axis_max` | integer | nil | X axis maximum | | `y_axis_min` | integer | nil | Y axis minimum | | `y_axis_max` | integer | nil | Y axis maximum | | `grid_lines` | boolean | true | Show grid lines | | `horizontal` | boolean | false | Horizontal bar chart | | `bar_width` | integer | nil | Bar width in pixels | | `bar_radius` | integer | nil | Bar border radius in pixels | | `extra_options` | map | `%{}` | Raw ECharts options deep-merged last (most powerful escape hatch) | --- ### 3. Real Template Usage Examples #### Line chart with time-series data and multi-series (xcode_overview_live.html.heex) This is the most representative pattern - used on virtually every analytics page: ```heex <.chart id="chart-single-multi-series-line" type="line" extra_options={ %{ grid: %{width: "93%", left: "0%", right: "7%", height: "88%", top: "5%"}, xAxis: %{ boundaryGap: false, type: "category", axisLabel: %{ color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary", formatter: "fn:toLocaleDate", customValues: [ @binary_cache_hit_rate_analytics.dates |> hd(), @binary_cache_hit_rate_analytics.dates |> List.last() ], padding: [10, 0, 0, 0] } }, yAxis: %{ splitLine: %{lineStyle: %{color: "var:noora-chart-lines"}}, axisLabel: %{ color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary", formatter: "{value}%" } }, tooltip: if @analytics_preset == "last-24-hours" do %{valueFormat: "{value}%", dateFormat: "hour"} else %{valueFormat: "{value}%"} end, legend: %{show: false} } } series={[ %{ data: Enum.zip(@binary_cache_hit_rate_analytics.dates, @binary_cache_hit_rate_analytics.values |> Enum.map(&(&1 * 100)) |> Enum.map(&Decimal.from_float/1) |> Enum.map(&Decimal.round(&1, 1))) |> Enum.map(&Tuple.to_list/1), name: dgettext("dashboard_projects", "Cache effectiveness"), type: "line", smooth: 0.1, symbol: "none" }, %{ data: Enum.zip(@selective_testing_analytics.dates, @selective_testing_analytics.values |> Enum.map(&(&1 * 100)) |> Enum.map(&Decimal.from_float/1) |> Enum.map(&Decimal.round(&1, 1))) |> Enum.map(&Tuple.to_list/1), name: dgettext("dashboard_projects", "Selective test effectiveness"), type: "line", smooth: 0.1, symbol: "none" } ]} y_axis_min={0} y_axis_max={100} /> ``` Key data shape: `series` is a list of maps, each with a `data` key containing `[[date_string, value], ...]` tuples. #### Horizontal stacked bar chart (build_run_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="cacheable-tasks-breakdown-chart" type="bar" extra_options={ %{ tooltip: %{trigger: "axis", axisPointer: %{type: "none"}}, legend: %{ left: "-0.3%", top: "bottom", orient: "horizontal", textStyle: %{color: "var:noora-surface-label-primary", fontFamily: "monospace", fontWeight: 400, fontSize: 10, lineHeight: 12}, icon: "path://M0 6C0 4.89543 0.895431 4 2 4H6C7.10457 4 8 4.89543 8 6C8 7.10457 7.10457 8 6 8H2C0.895431 8 0 7.10457 0 6Z", itemWidth: 8, itemHeight: 4 }, grid: %{width: "99%", left: "0%", height: "60%", top: "0%"}, xAxis: %{type: "value", axisLabel: %{show: false}, splitLine: %{show: false}}, yAxis: %{type: "category", data: [dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Cacheable tasks")], axisLabel: %{show: false}} } } series={ local_hits = @run.cacheable_task_local_hits_count remote_hits = @run.cacheable_task_remote_hits_count misses = @run.cacheable_tasks_count - remote_hits - local_hits [ %{name: dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Local"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{focus: "series"}, data: [local_hits], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-local", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, :local)}}, %{name: dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Remote"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{focus: "series"}, data: [remote_hits], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-remote", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, :remote)}}, %{name: dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Missed"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{focus: "series"}, data: [misses], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-missed", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, :misses)}} ] } x_axis_min={0} x_axis_max={@run.cacheable_tasks_count} /> ``` #### Bar chart with per-item colors and `bar_width`/`bar_radius` (xcode_overview_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="chart-recent-build-runs" type="bar" extra_options={ %{ grid: %{width: "100%", left: "0.4%", height: "88%", top: "5%"}, tooltip: %{valueFormat: "fn:formatSeconds", dateFormat: "minute"}, xAxis: %{ axisLabel: %{show: false}, data: @recent_build_runs |> Enum.map(& &1.date) }, yAxis: %{ splitLine: %{lineStyle: %{color: "var:noora-chart-lines"}}, axisLabel: %{color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary", formatter: "fn:formatSeconds"} }, legend: %{show: false} } } series={[%{data: @recent_build_runs, name: "Build", type: "bar"}]} y_axis_min={0} grid_lines bar_width={8} bar_radius={2} /> ``` Here `@recent_build_runs` is a list of maps shaped in the LiveView `.ex` file as: ```elixir %{value: value, itemStyle: %{color: color}, date: run.ran_at} ``` This lets each bar have its own color (`"var:noora-chart-primary"` for success, `"var:noora-chart-destructive"` for failure). #### Sunburst chart - bundle size analysis (bundle_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="bundle-size-analysis-sunburst" colors={["var:noora-sunburst-directory"]} type="line" series={[@series]} /> ``` The `@series` here contains the full ECharts sunburst series config (passed through directly since the series item is a map). The associated JavaScript hook `BundleSizeSunburstChartLegend` gets a reference to the underlying ECharts instance via `hook.chart` on the `.noora-chart` container to call `echart.dispatchAction()`, `echart.getOption()`, and respond to `mouseover`/`click` events. #### Flaky tests line chart (flaky_tests_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="flaky-runs-chart" type="line" extra_options={ %{ grid: %{width: "97%", left: "0.4%", height: "88%", top: "5%"}, xAxis: %{ boundaryGap: false, type: "category", axisLabel: %{ color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary", formatter: "fn:toLocaleDate", customValues: [ @flaky_runs_analytics.dates |> List.first(), @flaky_runs_analytics.dates |> List.last() ], padding: [10, 0, 0, 0] } }, yAxis: %{ splitNumber: 4, splitLine: %{lineStyle: %{color: "var:noora-chart-lines"}}, axisLabel: %{color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary"} }, legend: %{show: false}, tooltip: if(@analytics_preset == "last-24-hours", do: %{dateFormat: "hour"}, else: %{}) } } series={[ %{ color: "var:noora-chart-flaky", data: Enum.zip(@flaky_runs_analytics.dates, @flaky_runs_analytics.values) |> Enum.map(&Tuple.to_list/1), name: dgettext("dashboard_tests", "Flaky runs"), type: "line", smooth: 0.1, symbol: "none" } ]} y_axis_min={0} /> ``` --- ### 4. Key Patterns **Series data formats used in this codebase:** - Time-series: `[[date_string, numeric_value], ...]` - produced by `Enum.zip(dates, values) |> Enum.map(&Tuple.to_list/1)` - Per-item colored bars: `[%{value: v, itemStyle: %{color: "var:..."}, date: datetime}, ...]` - x-axis dates are passed separately via `extra_options.xAxis.data` - Stacked single-value bars: `[%{name: "...", type: "bar", stack: "total", data: [single_value], color: "var:...", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: [...]}}]` - Sunburst: A full ECharts series map passed as the lone element in the list **Built-in formatter strings used:** - `"fn:toLocaleDate"` - date formatting for x-axis labels - `"fn:formatSeconds"` - seconds to human time - `"fn:formatMilliseconds"` - milliseconds to human time - `"fn:formatHours"` - hours to human time - `"fn:formatBytes"` - bytes to human size - `"{value}%"` - ECharts native template for percentage labels **CSS variable color tokens used:** - `"var:noora-chart-primary"`, `"var:noora-chart-secondary"`, `"var:noora-chart-tertiary"` - `"var:noora-chart-destructive"`, `"var:noora-chart-flaky"`, `"var:noora-chart-warning"` - `"var:noora-chart-p99"`, `"var:noora-chart-p90"`, `"var:noora-chart-p50"` - `"var:noora-chart-lines"` (grid/split line color) - `"var:hits-chart-legend-local"`, `"var:hits-chart-legend-remote"`, `"var:hits-chart-legend-missed"` - `"var:noora-surface-label-secondary"` (axis label text color) **Files containing `.chart` usage:** - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/xcode_overview_live.html.heex` (6 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/tests_live.html.heex` (6 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/build_run_live.html.heex` (2 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/xcode_cache_live.html.heex` (3 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/bundle_live.html.heex` (1 sunburst chart) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/flaky_tests_live.html.heex` (1 chart) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/test_runs_live.html.heex` - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/gradle_overview_live.html.heex` - %{show: false}}, yAxis: %{type: "category", data: [dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Cacheable tasks")], axisLabel: %{show: false}} } } series={ local_hits = @run.cacheable_task_local_hits_count remote_hits = @run.cacheable_task_remote_hits_count misses = @run.cacheable_tasks_count - remote_hits - local_hits [ %{name: dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Local"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{focus: "series"}, data: [local_hits], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-local", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, :local)}}, %{name: dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Remote"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{focus: "series"}, data: [remote_hits], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-remote", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, :remote)}}, %{name: dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Missed"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{focus: "series"}, data: [misses], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-missed", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, :misses)}} ] } x_axis_min={0} x_axis_max={@run.cacheable_tasks_count} /> ``` #### Bar chart with per-item colors and `bar_width`/`bar_radius` (xcode_overview_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="chart-recent-build-runs" type="bar" extra_options={ %{ grid: %{width: "100%", left: "0.4%", height: "88%", top: "5%"}, tooltip: %{valueFormat: "fn:formatSeconds", dateFormat: "minute"}, xAxis: %{ axisLabel: %{show: false}, data: @recent_build_runs |> Enum.map(& &1.date) }, yAxis: %{ splitLine: %{lineStyle: %{color: "var:noora-chart-lines"}}, axisLabel: %{color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary", formatter: "fn:formatSeconds"} }, legend: %{show: false} } } series={[%{data: @recent_build_runs, name: "Build", type: "bar"}]} y_axis_min={0} grid_lines bar_width={8} bar_radius={2} /> ``` Here `@recent_build_runs` is a list of maps shaped in the LiveView `.ex` file as: ```elixir %{value: value, itemStyle: %{color: color}, date: run.ran_at} ``` This lets each bar have its own color (`"var:noora-chart-primary"` for success, `"var:noora-chart-destructive"` for failure). #### Sunburst chart - bundle size analysis (bundle_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="bundle-size-analysis-sunburst" colors={["var:noora-sunburst-directory"]} type="line" series={[@series]} /> ``` The `@series` here contains the full ECharts sunburst series config (passed through directly since the series item is a map). The associated JavaScript hook `BundleSizeSunburstChartLegend` gets a reference to the underlying ECharts instance via `hook.chart` on the `.noora-chart` container to call `echart.dispatchAction()`, `echart.getOption()`, and respond to `mouseover`/`click` events. #### Flaky tests line chart (flaky_tests_live.html.heex) ```heex <.chart id="flaky-runs-chart" type="line" extra_options={ %{ grid: %{width: "97%", left: "0.4%", height: "88%", top: "5%"}, xAxis: %{ boundaryGap: false, type: "category", axisLabel: %{ color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary", formatter: "fn:toLocaleDate", customValues: [ @flaky_runs_analytics.dates |> List.first(), @flaky_runs_analytics.dates |> List.last() ], padding: [10, 0, 0, 0] } }, yAxis: %{ splitNumber: 4, splitLine: %{lineStyle: %{color: "var:noora-chart-lines"}}, axisLabel: %{color: "var:noora-surface-label-secondary"} }, legend: %{show: false}, tooltip: if(@analytics_preset == "last-24-hours", do: %{dateFormat: "hour"}, else: %{}) } } series={[ %{ color: "var:noora-chart-flaky", data: Enum.zip(@flaky_runs_analytics.dates, @flaky_runs_analytics.values) |> Enum.map(&Tuple.to_list/1), name: dgettext("dashboard_tests", "Flaky runs"), type: "line", smooth: 0.1, symbol: "none" } ]} y_axis_min={0} /> ``` --- ### 4. Key Patterns **Series data formats used in this codebase:** - Time-series: `[[date_string, numeric_value], ...]` - produced by `Enum.zip(dates, values) |> Enum.map(&Tuple.to_list/1)` - Per-item colored bars: `[%{value: v, itemStyle: %{color: "var:..."}, date: datetime}, ...]` - x-axis dates are passed separately via `extra_options.xAxis.data` - Stacked single-value bars: `[%{name: "...", type: "bar", stack: "total", data: [single_value], color: "var:...", itemStyle: %{borderRadius: [...]}}]` - Sunburst: A full ECharts series map passed as the lone element in the list **Built-in formatter strings used:** - `"fn:toLocaleDate"` - date formatting for x-axis labels - `"fn:formatSeconds"` - seconds to human time - `"fn:formatMilliseconds"` - milliseconds to human time - `"fn:formatHours"` - hours to human time - `"fn:formatBytes"` - bytes to human size - `"{value}%"` - ECharts native template for percentage labels **CSS variable color tokens used:** - `"var:noora-chart-primary"`, `"var:noora-chart-secondary"`, `"var:noora-chart-tertiary"` - `"var:noora-chart-destructive"`, `"var:noora-chart-flaky"`, `"var:noora-chart-warning"` - `"var:noora-chart-p99"`, `"var:noora-chart-p90"`, `"var:noora-chart-p50"` - `"var:noora-chart-lines"` (grid/split line color) - `"var:hits-chart-legend-local"`, `"var:hits-chart-legend-remote"`, `"var:hits-chart-legend-missed"` - `"var:noora-surface-label-secondary"` (axis label text color) **Files containing `.chart` usage:** - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/xcode_overview_live.html.heex` (6 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/tests_live.html.heex` (6 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/build_run_live.html.heex` (2 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/xcode_cache_live.html.heex` (3 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/test_cases_live.html.heex` (2 charts) - `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/bundles_live.html.heex` (1 chart) - scope "/builds" do
          get "/", BuildsController, :index
          get "/:build_id", BuildsController, :show
          post "/", BuildsController, :create
        end

        scope "/gradle" do
          post "/builds", GradleController, :create_build
          get "/builds", GradleController, :list_builds
          get "/builds/:build_id", GradleController, :get_build
        end

        scope "/previews" do
          post "/start", PreviewsController, :multipart_start
          post "/generate-url", PreviewsController, :multipart_generate_url
          post "/complete", PreviewsController, :multipart_complete
          post "/:preview_id/icons", PreviewsController, :upload_icon
          get "/latest", PreviewsController, :latest
          get "/:preview_id", PreviewsController, :show
          get "/", PreviewsController, :index

1→defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→  use TuistWeb, :live_view
     4→  use Noora
     5→
     6→  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge
     7→
     8→  alias Noora.Filter
     9→  alias Tuist.Gradle
    10→  alias Tuist.Repo
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests
    12→  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
    13→  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
    14→  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
    15→  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
    16→  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query
    17→
    18→  @table_page_size 25
    19→
    20→  @doc """
    21→  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
    22→  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
    23→  """
    24→  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    25→    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns
    26→
    27→    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
    28→      {:error, :not_found} ->
    29→        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
    30→
    31→      {:ok, build} ->
    32→        if build.project_id != project.id do
    33→          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
    34→        end
    35→
    36→        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    37→    end
    38→  end
    39→
    40→  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    41→    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)
    42→
    43→    test_run =
    44→      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
    45→        {:ok, test} -> test
    46→        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
    47→      end
    48→
    49→    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    50→    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)
    51→
    52→    download_throughput =
    53→      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
    54→        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
    55→        else: 0
    56→
    57→    upload_throughput =
    58→      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
    59→        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
    60→        else: 0
    61→
    62→    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    63→    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")
    64→
    65→    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    66→    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    67→    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    68→    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0
    69→
    70→    socket
    71→    |> assign(:build, build)
    72→    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    73→    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    74→    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    75→    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    76→    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)
    77→    |> assign(:cache_hit_rate, Gradle.cache_hit_rate(build))
    78→    |> assign(:cache_download_bytes, aggregates.cache_download_bytes)
    79→    |> assign(:cache_upload_bytes, aggregates.cache_upload_bytes)
    80→    |> assign(:download_throughput, download_throughput)
    81→    |> assign(:upload_throughput, upload_throughput)
    82→    |> assign(:title, title)
    83→    |> assign(:head_title, "#{title} · #{slug} · Tuist")
    84→  end
    85→
    86→  @doc """
    87→  Assigns gradle build tab data to the socket during handle_params.
    88→  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
    89→  """
    90→  def assign_handle_params(socket, params) do
    91→    selected_tab = params["tab"] || "overview"
    92→    uri = URI.new!("?" <> URI.encode_query(params))
    93→
    94→    socket
    95→    |> assign(:selected_tab, selected_tab)
    96→    |> assign(:uri, uri)
    97→    |> assign_tab_data(selected_tab, params)
    98→  end
    99→
   100→  defp build_run_path(socket) do
   101→    %{selected_account: account, selected_project: project, build: build} = socket.assigns
   102→    "/#{account.name}/#{project.name}/builds/build-runs/#{build.id}"
   103→  end
   104→
   105→  def handle_event("search-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
   106→    query =
   107→      socket.assigns.uri.query
   108→      |> Query.put("tasks-filter", search)
   109→      |> Query.put("tasks-page", "1")
   110→
   111→    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
   112→  end
   113→
   114→  def handle_event("search-cacheable-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
   115→    query =
   116→      socket.assigns.uri.query
   117→      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-filter", search)
   118→      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-page", "1")
   119→
   120→    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
   121→  end
   122→
   123→  def handle_event("add_filter", %{"value" => filter_id}, socket) do
   124→    page_param =
   125→      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
   126→        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
   127→        else: "tasks-page"
   128→
   129→    updated_params =
   130→      filter_id
   131→      |> Filter.Operations.add_filter_to_query(socket)
   132→      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")
   133→
   134→    {:noreply,
   135→     socket
   136→     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_params)}")
   137→     |> push_event("open-dropdown", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-dropdown"})
   138→     |> push_event("open-popover", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-popover"})}
   139→  end
   140→
   141→  def handle_event("update_filter", params, socket) do
   142→    page_param =
   143→      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
   144→        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
   145→        else: "tasks-page"
   146→
   147→    updated_query_params =
   148→      params
   149→      |> Filter.Operations.update_filters_in_query(socket)
   150→      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")
   151→
   152→    {:noreply,
   153→     socket
   154→     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_query_params)}")
   155→     |> push_event("close-dropdown", %{id: "all", all: true})
   156→     |> push_event("close-popover", %{id: "all", all: true})}
   157→  end
   158→
   159→  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "overview", params) do
   160→    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id
   161→
   162→    filters = Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_task_filters())
   163→    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["tasks-filter"])
   164→    dropdown_filters = build_task_flop_filters(filters)
   165→    sort_by = ensure_allowed_sort_by(params["tasks-sort-by"])
   166→    sort_order = params["tasks-sort-order"] || "asc"
   167→
   168→    flop_params = %{
   169→      filters: text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
   170→      page: String.to_integer(params["tasks-page"] || "1"),
   171→      page_size: @table_page_size,
   172→      order_by: [sort_by],
   173→      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
   174→    }
   175→
   176→    {tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)
   177→
   178→    socket
   179→    |> assign(:tasks, tasks)
   180→    |> assign(:tasks_page, meta.current_page)
   181→    |> assign(:tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
   182→    |> assign(:tasks_filter, params["tasks-filter"] || "")
   183→    |> assign(:tasks_sort_by, params["tasks-sort-by"] || "started_at")
   184→    |> assign(:tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
   185→    |> assign(:tasks_active_filters, filters)
   186→    |> assign(:available_filters, define_task_filters())
   187→  end
   188→
   189→  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "gradle-cache", params) do
   190→    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id
   191→
   192→    filters =
   193→      Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_cacheable_task_filters())
   194→
   195→    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["cacheable-tasks-filter"])
   196→    dropdown_filters = build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters)
   197→    sort_by = ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"])
   198→    sort_order = params["cacheable-tasks-sort-order"] || "desc"
   199→
   200→    flop_params = %{
   201→      filters: [%{field: :cacheable, op: :==, value: true}] ++ text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
   202→      page: String.to_integer(params["cacheable-tasks-page"] || "1"),
   203→      page_size: @table_page_size,
   204→      order_by: [sort_by],
   205→      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
   206→    }
   207→
   208→    {cacheable_tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)
   209→
   210→    socket
   211→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks, cacheable_tasks)
   212→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page, meta.current_page)
   213→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
   214→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_filter, params["cacheable-tasks-filter"] || "")
   215→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_by, params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"] || "duration_ms")
   216→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
   217→    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_active_filters, filters)
   218→    |> assign(:available_filters, define_cacheable_task_filters())
   219→  end
   220→
   221→  defp assign_tab_data(socket, _tab, _params), do: socket
   222→
   223→  defp define_task_filters do
   224→    [
   225→      %Filter.Filter{

defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
  @moduledoc false
  use TuistWeb, :live_view
  use Noora

  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge

  alias Noora.Filter
  alias Tuist.Gradle
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.Tests
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query

  @table_page_size 25

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns

    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
      {:error, :not_found} ->
        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")

      {:ok, build} ->
        if build.project_id != project.id do
          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
        end

        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    end
  end

  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)

    test_run =
      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
        {:ok, test} -> test
        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
      end

    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)

    download_throughput =
      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    upload_throughput =
      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")

    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0

    socket
    |> assign(:build, build)
    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)
    |> assign(:cache_hit_rate, Gradle.cache_hit_rate(build))
    |> assign(:cache_download_bytes, aggregates.cache_download_bytes)
    |> assign(:cache_upload_bytes, aggregates.cache_upload_bytes)
    |> assign(:download_throughput, download_throughput)
    |> assign(:upload_throughput, upload_throughput)
    |> assign(:title, title)
    |> assign(:head_title, "#{title} · #{slug} · Tuist")
  end

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build tab data to the socket during handle_params.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_handle_params(socket, params) do
    selected_tab = params["tab"] || "overview"
    uri = URI.new!("?" <> URI.encode_query(params))

    socket
    |> assign(:selected_tab, selected_tab)
    |> assign(:uri, uri)
    |> assign_tab_data(selected_tab, params)
  end

  defp build_run_path(socket) do
    %{selected_account: account, selected_project: project, build: build} = socket.assigns
    "/#{account.name}/#{project.name}/builds/build-runs/#{build.id}"
  end

  def handle_event("search-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("search-cacheable-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("add_filter", %{"value" => filter_id}, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_params =
      filter_id
      |> Filter.Operations.add_filter_to_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_params)}")
     |> push_event("open-dropdown", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-dropdown"})
     |> push_event("open-popover", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-popover"})}
  end

  def handle_event("update_filter", params, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_query_params =
      params
      |> Filter.Operations.update_filters_in_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_query_params)}")
     |> push_event("close-dropdown", %{id: "all", all: true})
     |> push_event("close-popover", %{id: "all", all: true})}
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "overview", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters = Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_task_filters())
    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_sort_by(params["tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["tasks-sort-order"] || "asc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:tasks, tasks)
    |> assign(:tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:tasks_filter, params["tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_by, params["tasks-sort-by"] || "started_at")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "gradle-cache", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters =
      Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_cacheable_task_filters())

    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["cacheable-tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["cacheable-tasks-sort-order"] || "desc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: [%{field: :cacheable, op: :==, value: true}] ++ text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["cacheable-tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {cacheable_tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks, cacheable_tasks)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_filter, params["cacheable-tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_by, params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"] || "duration_ms")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_cacheable_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, _tab, _params), do: socket

  defp define_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Outcome"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :up_to_date, :executed, :failed, :skipped, :no_source],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit"),
          up_to_date: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed"),
          failed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed"),
          skipped: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped"),
          no_source: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_text_flop_filters(nil), do: []
  defp build_text_flop_filters(""), do: []

  defp build_text_flop_filters(filter_text) do
    [%{field: :task_path, op: :like, value: filter_text}]
  end

  defp build_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(value) when value in ["task_path", "duration_ms", "started_at"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(_), do: :started_at

  defp define_cacheable_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Status"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :executed],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Missed")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(value) when value in ["duration_ms", "cache_artifact_size"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(_), do: :duration_ms

  def sort_icon("desc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_down"
  def sort_icon("asc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_up"

  def sort_order_patch_value(category, current_category, current_order) do
    if category == current_category do
      if current_order == "asc", do: "desc", else: "asc"
    else
      "asc"
    end
  end

  defp outcome_color("local_hit"), do: "success"
  defp outcome_color("remote_hit"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("up_to_date"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("executed"), do: "secondary"
  defp outcome_color("failed"), do: "destructive"
  defp outcome_color(_), do: "secondary"

  defp outcome_label("local_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit")
  defp outcome_label("remote_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit")
  defp outcome_label("up_to_date"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date")
  defp outcome_label("executed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed")
  defp outcome_label("failed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed")
  defp outcome_label("skipped"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped")
  defp outcome_label("no_source"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
  defp outcome_label(other), do: other

  defp format_duration(duration_ms) do
    DateFormatter.format_duration_from_milliseconds(duration_ms)
  end

  defp started_after(task, build_started_at) do
    if task.started_at && build_started_at do
      diff_ms = NaiveDateTime.diff(task.started_at, build_started_at, :millisecond)
      format_started_after_ms(max(diff_ms, 0))
    else
      dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "—")
    end
  end

  defp format_started_after_ms(0), do: "0.00s"
  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) when ms < 1000, do: "#{ms}ms"

  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) do
    hours = div(ms, 3_600_000)
    remainder = rem(ms, 3_600_000)
    minutes = div(remainder, 60_000)
    remainder = rem(remainder, 60_000)
    seconds = div(remainder, 1_000)
    millis = rem(remainder, 1_000)

    parts = []
    parts = if hours > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{hours}h"], else: parts
    parts = if minutes > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{minutes}m"], else: parts

    parts =
      if ms > 60_000 and seconds > 0 do
        parts ++ ["#{seconds}s"]
      else
        seconds_with_ms = seconds + millis / 1000
        parts ++ [:erlang.float_to_binary(Float.round(seconds_with_ms, 2), decimals: 2) <> "s"]
      end

    Enum.join(parts, " ")
  end
end

1→defmodule TuistWeb.BuildRunLive do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→  use TuistWeb, :live_view
     4→  use Noora
     5→
     6→  import Phoenix.Component
     7→  import TuistWeb.PercentileDropdownWidget
     8→  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge
     9→
    10→  alias Noora.Filter
    11→  alias Tuist.Builds
    12→  alias Tuist.Builds.CASOutput
    13→  alias Tuist.CommandEvents
    14→  alias Tuist.Projects
    15→  alias Tuist.Projects.Project
    16→  alias Tuist.Tests
    17→  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
    18→  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query
    19→
    20→  def mount(params, session, %{assigns: %{selected_project: project}} = socket) when is_struct(project) do
    21→    if Project.gradle_project?(project) do
    22→      mount_gradle(params, session, socket)
    23→    else
    24→      mount_xcode(params, session, socket)
    25→    end
    26→  end
    27→
    28→  defp mount_gradle(params, _session, socket) do
    29→    socket = TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive.assign_mount(socket, params["build_run_id"])
    30→    {:ok, socket}
    31→  end
    32→
    33→  # credo:disable-for-next-line Credo.Check.Refactor.CyclomaticComplexity
    34→  defp mount_xcode(params, _session, %{assigns: %{selected_project: project}} = socket) do
    35→    run =
    36→      case Builds.get_build(params["build_run_id"]) do
    37→        nil ->
    38→          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Build not found.")
    39→
    40→        run ->
    41→          run
    42→      end
    43→
    44→    slug = Projects.get_project_slug_from_id(project.id)
    45→
    46→    run =
    47→      run
    48→      |> Tuist.Repo.preload([:ran_by_account, project: :vcs_connection])
    49→      |> Tuist.ClickHouseRepo.preload([:issues])
    50→
    51→    command_event =
    52→      case CommandEvents.get_command_event_by_build_run_id(run.id) do
    53→        {:ok, event} -> event
    54→        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
    55→      end
    56→
    57→    if run.project.id != project.id do
    58→      raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Build not found.")
    59→    end
    60→
    61→    cas_metrics = Builds.cas_output_metrics(run.id)
    62→    cacheable_task_latency_metrics = Builds.cacheable_task_latency_metrics(run.id)
    63→
    64→    test_run =
    65→      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_build_run_id(run.id) do
    66→        {:ok, test} -> test
    67→        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
    68→      end
    69→
    70→    socket =
    71→      socket
    72→      |> assign(:run, run)
    73→      |> assign(:command_event, command_event)
    74→      |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    75→      |> assign(:cas_metrics, cas_metrics)
    76→      |> assign(:cacheable_task_latency_metrics, cacheable_task_latency_metrics)
    77→      |> assign(:head_title, "#{dgettext("dashboard_builds", "Build Run")} · #{slug} · Tuist")
    78→      |> assign(
    79→        :warnings_grouped_by_path,
    80→        run.issues |> Enum.filter(&(&1.type == "warning")) |> Enum.group_by(& &1.path)
    81→      )
    82→      |> assign(
    83→        :errors_grouped_by_path,
    84→        run.issues |> Enum.filter(&(&1.type == "error")) |> Enum.group_by(& &1.path)
    85→      )
    86→      |> assign(:file_breakdown_available_filters, define_file_breakdown_filters())
    87→      |> assign(:file_breakdown_active_filters, [])
    88→      |> assign(:module_breakdown_available_filters, define_module_breakdown_filters())
    89→      |> assign(:module_breakdown_active_filters, [])
    90→      |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_available_filters, define_cacheable_tasks_filters())
    91→      |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_active_filters, [])
    92→      |> assign(:cas_outputs_available_filters, define_cas_outputs_filters())
    93→      |> assign(:cas_outputs_active_filters, [])
    94→      |> assign(:selected_read_latency_type, "avg")
    95→      |> assign(:selected_write_latency_type, "avg")
    96→      |> assign(:expanded_task_keys, MapSet.new())
    97→      |> assign(:task_cas_outputs_map, %{})
    98→      |> assign_async(:has_result_bundle, fn ->
    99→        {:ok, %{has_result_bundle: (command_event && CommandEvents.has_result_bundle?(command_event)) || false}}
   100→      end)
   101→
   102→    {:ok, socket}
   103→  end

   105→  def handle_params(params, _uri, %{assigns: %{selected_project: project}} = socket) do
   106→    if Project.gradle_project?(project) do
   107→      {:noreply, TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive.assign_handle_params(socket, params)}
   108→    else
   109→      handle_params_xcode(params, socket)
   110→    end
   111→  end
   112→
   113→  # credo:disable-for-next-line Credo.Check.Refactor.CyclomaticComplexity
   114→  defp handle_params_xcode(
   115→         params,
   116→         %{
   117→           assigns: %{
   118→             file_breakdown_available_filters: file_breakdown_available_filters,
   119→             file_breakdown_active_filters: file_breakdown_active_filters,
   120→             module_breakdown_available_filters: module_breakdown_available_filters,
   121→             module_breakdown_active_filters: module_breakdown_active_filters,
   122→             cacheable_tasks_available_filters: cacheable_tasks_available_filters,
   123→             cacheable_tasks_active_filters: cacheable_tasks_active_filters,
   124→             cas_outputs_available_filters: cas_outputs_available_filters,
   125→             cas_outputs_active_filters: cas_outputs_active_filters
   126→           }
   127→         } = socket
   128→       ) do
   129→    uri = URI.new!("?" <> URI.encode_query(params))
   130→    selected_breakdown_tab = params["breakdown-tab"] || "module"
   131→    selected_cache_tab = params["cache-tab"] || "cacheable-tasks"
   132→
   133→    selected_tab = params["tab"] || "overview"
   134→
   135→    available_filters =
   136→      case {selected_tab, selected_breakdown_tab, selected_cache_tab} do
   137→        {"overview", "file", _} -> file_breakdown_available_filters
   138→        {"overview", "module", _} -> module_breakdown_available_filters
   139→        {"xcode-cache", _, "cacheable-tasks"} -> cacheable_tasks_available_filters
   140→        {"xcode-cache", _, "cas-outputs"} -> cas_outputs_available_filters
   141→        _ -> []
   142→      end
   143→
   144→    active_filters =
   145→      case {selected_tab, selected_breakdown_tab, selected_cache_tab} do
   146→        {"overview", "file", _} -> file_breakdown_active_filters
   147→        {"overview", "module", _} -> module_breakdown_active_filters
   148→        {"xcode-cache", _, "cacheable-tasks"} -> cacheable_tasks_active_filters
   149→        {"xcode-cache", _, "cas-outputs"} -> cas_outputs_active_filters
   150→        _ -> []
   151→      end
   152→
   153→    selected_read_latency_type = params["read-latency-type"] || "avg"
   154→    selected_write_latency_type = params["write-latency-type"] || "avg"
   155→
   156→    socket =
   157→      socket
   158→      |> assign(:selected_tab, selected_tab)
   159→      |> assign(:uri, uri)
   160→      |> assign(:available_filters, available_filters)
   161→      |> assign(:active_filters, active_filters)
   162→      |> assign(:selected_read_latency_type, selected_read_latency_type)
   163→      |> assign(:selected_write_latency_type, selected_write_latency_type)
   164→      |> assign_file_breakdown(params)
   165→      |> assign_module_breakdown(params)
   166→      |> assign_cacheable_tasks(params)
   167→      |> assign_cas_outputs(params)
   168→      |> assign(:selected_breakdown_tab, selected_breakdown_tab)
   169→      |> assign(:selected_cache_tab, selected_cache_tab)
   170→
   171→    {
   172→      :noreply,
   173→      socket
   174→    }
   175→  end

   178→      when event in ["search-tasks", "search-cacheable-tasks", "add_filter", "update_filter"] and is_struct(project) do
   179→    if Project.gradle_project?(project) do
   180→      TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive.handle_event(event, params, socket)
   181→    else
   182→      handle_event_xcode(event, params, socket)
   183→    end
   184→  end

   320→  defp file_breakdown_filters(run, params, available_filters, search) do
   321→    base_filters =
   322→      [
   323→        %{field: :build_run_id, op: :==, value: run.id}
   324→      ] ++
   325→        Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop(Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, available_filters))
   326→
   327→    if search && search != "" do
   328→      base_filters ++
   329→        [
   330→          %{field: :path, op: :like, value: search}
   331→        ]
   332→    else
   333→      base_filters
   334→    end
   335→  end
   336→
   337→  defp module_breakdown_filters(run, params, available_filters, search) do
   338→    base_filters =
   339→      [%{field: :build_run_id, op: :==, value: run.id}] ++
   340→        Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop(Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, available_filters))
   341→
   342→    if search && search != "" do
   343→      base_filters ++
   344→        [
   345→          %{field: :name, op: :like, value: search}
   346→        ]
   347→    else
   348→      base_filters
   349→    end
   350→  end
   351→
   352→  defp file_breakdown_order_by(sort_by) do
   353→    case sort_by do
   354→      "compilation-duration" -> [:compilation_duration]
   355→      "file" -> [:path]
   356→      _ -> [:compilation_duration]
   357→    end
   358→  end
   359→
   360→  defp assign_file_breakdown(
   361→         %{assigns: %{run: run, file_breakdown_available_filters: available_filters}} = socket,
   362→         params
   363→       ) do
   364→    file_breakdown_search = params["file-breakdown-search"] || ""
   365→    file_breakdown_sort_by = params["file-breakdown-sort-by"] || "compilation-duration"
   366→
   367→    default_sort_order =
   368→      case file_breakdown_sort_by do
   369→        "file" -> "asc"
   370→        _ -> "desc"
   371→      end
   372→
   373→    file_breakdown_sort_order = params["file-breakdown-sort-order"] || default_sort_order
   374→
   375→    file_breakdown_page =
   376→      params["file-breakdown-page"]
   377→      |> to_string()
   378→      |> Integer.parse()
   379→      |> case do
   380→        {int, _} -> int
   381→        :error -> 1
   382→      end
   383→
   384→    flop_filters = file_breakdown_filters(run, params, available_filters, file_breakdown_search)
   385→
   386→    order_by = file_breakdown_order_by(file_breakdown_sort_by)
   387→
   388→    order_directions = map_sort_order(file_breakdown_sort_order)
   389→
   390→    options = %{
   391→      filters: flop_filters,
   392→      order_by: order_by,
   393→      order_directions: order_directions,
   394→      page: file_breakdown_page,
   395→      page_size: 20
   396→    }
   397→
   398→    {files, files_meta} = Builds.list_build_files(options)
   399→
   400→    filters =
   401→      Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, available_filters)
   402→
   403→    socket
   404→    |> assign(:file_breakdown_search, file_breakdown_search)
   405→    |> assign(
   406→      :file_breakdown_files,
   407→      files
   408→    )
   409→    |> assign(:file_breakdown_page, file_breakdown_page)
   410→    |> assign(:file_breakdown_sort_by, file_breakdown_sort_by)
   411→    |> assign(:file_breakdown_sort_order, file_breakdown_sort_order)
   412→    |> assign(:file_breakdown_files_meta, files_meta)
   413→    |> assign(:file_breakdown_active_filters, filters)
   414→  end
   415→
   416→  defp map_sort_order(sort_order) do
   417→    case sort_order do
   418→      "asc" -> [:asc]
   419→      "desc" -> [:desc]
   420→      _ -> [:desc]
   421→    end
   422→  end
   423→
   424→  defp module_breakdown_order_by(module_breakdown_sort_by) do
   425→    case module_breakdown_sort_by do
   426→      "build-duration" -> [:build_duration]
   427→      "compilation-duration" -> [:compilation_duration]
   428→      "name" -> [:name]
   429→      _ -> [:name]
   430→    end
   431→  end
   432→
   433→  defp cacheable_tasks_order_by(cacheable_tasks_sort_by) do
   434→    case cacheable_tasks_sort_by do
   435→      "description" -> [:description]
   436→      "key" -> [:key]
   437→      _ -> [:description]
   438→    end
   439→  end

   444→       ) do
   445→    module_breakdown_search = params["module-breakdown-search"] || ""

   448→    default_sort_order =

   451→        _ -> "desc"
   452→      end
   453→
   454→    module_breakdown_sort_order = params["module-breakdown-sort-order"] || default_sort_order

   456→    module_breakdown_page =
