defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
  @moduledoc false
  use TuistWeb, :live_view
  use Noora

  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge

  alias Noora.Filter
  alias Tuist.Gradle
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.Tests
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query

  @table_page_size 25

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns

    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
      {:error, :not_found} ->
        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")

      {:ok, build} ->
        if build.project_id != project.id do
          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
        end

        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    end
  end

  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)

    test_run =
      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
        {:ok, test} -> test
        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
      end

    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)

    download_throughput =
      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    upload_throughput =
      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")

    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0

    socket
    |> assign(:build, build)
    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)
    |> assign(:cache_hit_rate, Gradle.cache_hit_rate(build))
    |> assign(:cache_download_bytes, aggregates.cache_download_bytes)
    |> assign(:cache_upload_bytes, aggregates.cache_upload_bytes)
    |> assign(:download_throughput, download_throughput)
    |> assign(:upload_throughput, upload_throughput)
    |> assign(:title, title)
    |> assign(:head_title, "#{title} · #{slug} · Tuist")
  end

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build tab data to the socket during handle_params.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_handle_params(socket, params) do
    selected_tab = params["tab"] || "overview"
    uri = URI.new!("?" <> URI.encode_query(params))

    socket
    |> assign(:selected_tab, selected_tab)
    |> assign(:uri, uri)
    |> assign_tab_data(selected_tab, params)
  end

  defp build_run_path(socket) do
    %{selected_account: account, selected_project: project, build: build} = socket.assigns
    "/#{account.name}/#{project.name}/builds/build-runs/#{build.id}"
  end

  def handle_event("search-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("search-cacheable-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("add_filter", %{"value" => filter_id}, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_params =
      filter_id
      |> Filter.Operations.add_filter_to_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_params)}")
     |> push_event("open-dropdown", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-dropdown"})
     |> push_event("open-popover", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-popover"})}
  end

  def handle_event("update_filter", params, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_query_params =
      params
      |> Filter.Operations.update_filters_in_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_query_params)}")
     |> push_event("close-dropdown", %{id: "all", all: true})
     |> push_event("close-popover", %{id: "all", all: true})}
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "overview", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters = Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_task_filters())
    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_sort_by(params["tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["tasks-sort-order"] || "asc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:tasks, tasks)
    |> assign(:tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:tasks_filter, params["tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_by, params["tasks-sort-by"] || "started_at")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "gradle-cache", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters =
      Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_cacheable_task_filters())

    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["cacheable-tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["cacheable-tasks-sort-order"] || "desc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: [%{field: :cacheable, op: :==, value: true}] ++ text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["cacheable-tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {cacheable_tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks, cacheable_tasks)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_filter, params["cacheable-tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_by, params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"] || "duration_ms")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_cacheable_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, _tab, _params), do: socket

  defp define_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Outcome"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :up_to_date, :executed, :failed, :skipped, :no_source],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit"),
          up_to_date: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed"),
          failed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed"),
          skipped: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped"),
          no_source: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_text_flop_filters(nil), do: []
  defp build_text_flop_filters(""), do: []

  defp build_text_flop_filters(filter_text) do
    [%{field: :task_path, op: :like, value: filter_text}]
  end

  defp build_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(value) when value in ["task_path", "duration_ms", "started_at"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(_), do: :started_at

  defp define_cacheable_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Status"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :executed],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Missed")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(value) when value in ["duration_ms", "cache_artifact_size"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(_), do: :duration_ms

  def sort_icon("desc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_down"
  def sort_icon("asc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_up"

  def sort_order_patch_value(category, current_category, current_order) do
    if category == current_category do
      if current_order == "asc", do: "desc", else: "asc"
    else
      "asc"
    end
  end

  defp outcome_color("local_hit"), do: "success"
  defp outcome_color("remote_hit"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("up_to_date"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("executed"), do: "secondary"
  defp outcome_color("failed"), do: "destructive"
  defp outcome_color(_), do: "secondary"

  defp outcome_label("local_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit")
  defp outcome_label("remote_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit")
  defp outcome_label("up_to_date"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date")
  defp outcome_label("executed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed")
  defp outcome_label("failed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed")
  defp outcome_label("skipped"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped")
  defp outcome_label("no_source"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
  defp outcome_label(other), do: other

  defp format_duration(duration_ms) do
    DateFormatter.format_duration_from_milliseconds(duration_ms)
  end

  defp started_after(task, build_started_at) do
    if task.started_at && build_started_at do
      diff_ms = NaiveDateTime.diff(task.started_at, build_started_at, :millisecond)
      format_started_after_ms(max(diff_ms, 0))
    else
      dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "—")
    end
  end

  defp format_started_after_ms(0), do: "0.00s"
  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) when ms < 1000, do: "#{ms}ms"

  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) do
    hours = div(ms, 3_600_000)
    remainder = rem(ms, 3_600_000)
    minutes = div(remainder, 60_000)
    remainder = rem(remainder, 60_000)
    seconds = div(remainder, 1_000)
    millis = rem(remainder, 1_000)

    parts = []
    parts = if hours > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{hours}h"], else: parts
    parts = if minutes > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{minutes}m"], else: parts

    parts =
      if ms > 60_000 and seconds > 0 do
        parts ++ ["#{seconds}s"]
      else
        seconds_with_ms = seconds + millis / 1000
        parts ++ [:erlang.float_to_binary(Float.round(seconds_with_ms, 2), decimals: 2) <> "s"]
      end

    Enum.join(parts, " ")
  end
end

defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
  @moduledoc false
  use TuistWeb, :live_view
  use Noora

  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge

  alias Noora.Filter
  alias Tuist.Gradle
  alias Tuist.Gradle.LiveTraces
  alias Tuist.Gradle.Waterfall
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.Tests
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query

  @table_page_size 25

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns

    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
      {:error, :not_found} ->
        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")

      {:ok, build} ->
        if build.project_id != project.id do
          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
        end

        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    end
  end

  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)

    test_run =
      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
        {:ok, test} -> test
        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
      end

    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)

    download_throughput =
      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    upload_throughput =
      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")

    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0

    socket
    |> assign(:build, build)
    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "overview", params) do

    dropdown_filters = build_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_sort_by(params["tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["tasks-sort-order"] || "asc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["tasks-page"] || "1"),

      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",

        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Outcome"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :up_to_date, :executed, :failed, :skipped, :no_source],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit"),

  end

    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp define_cacheable_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{

        field: :outcome,

        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :executed],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote"),

    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(_), do: :duration_ms

  def sort_icon("desc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_down"
  def sort_icon("asc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_up"

  def sort_order_patch_value(category, current_category, current_order) do
    if category == current_category do
      if current_order == "asc", do: "desc", else: "asc"
    else
      "asc"
    end
  end

  defp outcome_color("local_hit"), do: "success"
  defp outcome_color("remote_hit"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("up_to_date"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("executed"), do: "secondary"
  defp outcome_color("failed"), do: "destructive"
  defp outcome_color(_), do: "secondary"

  defp outcome_label("local_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit")
  defp outcome_label("remote_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit")
  defp outcome_label("up_to_date"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date")
  defp outcome_label("executed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed")
  defp outcome_label("failed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed")
  defp outcome_label("skipped"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped")
  defp outcome_label("no_source"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
  defp outcome_label(other), do: other

  defp format_duration(duration_ms) do
    DateFormatter.format_duration_from_milliseconds(duration_ms)
  end

  defp started_after(task, build_started_at) do
    if task.started_at && build_started_at do
      diff_ms = NaiveDateTime.diff(task.started_at, build_started_at, :millisecond)
      format_started_after_ms(max(diff_ms, 0))
    else
      dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "—")
    end
  end

  defp format_started_after_ms(0), do: "0.00s"
  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) when ms < 1000, do: "#{ms}ms"

  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) do
    hours = div(ms, 3_600_000)
    remainder = rem(ms, 3_600_000)
    minutes = div(remainder, 60_000)
    remainder = rem(remainder, 60_000)
    seconds = div(remainder, 1_000)
    millis = rem(remainder, 1_000)

    parts = []
    parts = if hours > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{hours}h"], else: parts
    parts = if minutes > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{minutes}m"], else: parts

    parts =
      if ms > 60_000 and seconds > 0 do
        parts ++ ["#{seconds}s"]
      else
        seconds_with_ms = seconds + millis / 1000
        parts ++ [:erlang.float_to_binary(Float.round(seconds_with_ms, 2), decimals: 2) <> "s"]
      end

    Enum.join(parts, " ")
  end
end

defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
  @moduledoc false
  use TuistWeb, :live_view
  use Noora

  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge

  alias Noora.Filter
  alias Tuist.Gradle
  alias Tuist.Gradle.LiveTraces
  alias Tuist.Gradle.Waterfall
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.Tests
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query

  @table_page_size 25

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns

    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
      {:error, :not_found} ->
        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")

      {:ok, build} ->
        if build.project_id != project.id do
          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
        end

        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    end
  end

  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)

    test_run =
      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
        {:ok, test} -> test
        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
      end

    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)

    download_throughput =
      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    upload_throughput =
      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")

    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0

    if Phoenix.LiveView.connected?(socket) do
      Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(Tuist.PubSub, "gradle_traces:#{project.id}:#{build.id}")
    end

    socket
    |> assign(:build, build)
    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)
    |> assign(:cache_hit_rate, Gradle.cache_hit_rate(build))
    |> assign(:cache_download_bytes, aggregates.cache_download_bytes)
    |> assign(:cache_upload_bytes, aggregates.cache_upload_bytes)
    |> assign(:download_throughput, download_throughput)
    |> assign(:upload_throughput, upload_throughput)
    |> assign(:title, title)
    |> assign(:head_title, "#{title} · #{slug} · Tuist")
    |> assign(:live_spans, [])
    |> assign(:waterfall_data, nil)
  end

  @doc """

  defp outcome_label("no_source"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
  defp outcome_label(other), do: other

  defp format_duration(duration_ms) do
    DateFormatter.format_duration_from_milliseconds(duration_ms)
  end

  defp started_after(task, build_started_at) do
    if task.started_at && build_started_at do
      diff_ms = NaiveDateTime.diff(task.started_at, build_started_at, :millisecond)
      format_started_after_ms(max(diff_ms, 0))
    else
      dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "—")
    end
  end

  defp format_started_after_ms(0), do: "0.00s"
  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) when ms < 1000, do: "#{ms}ms"

  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) do
    hours = div(ms, 3_600_000)
    remainder = rem(ms, 3_600_000)
    minutes = div(remainder, 60_000)
    remainder = rem(remainder, 60_000)
    seconds = div(remainder, 1_000)
    millis = rem(remainder, 1_000)

    parts = []
    parts = if hours > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{hours}h"], else: parts
    parts = if minutes > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{minutes}m"], else: parts

    parts =
      if ms > 60_000 and seconds > 0 do
        parts ++ ["#{seconds}s"]
      else
        seconds_with_ms = seconds + millis / 1000
        parts ++ [:erlang.float_to_binary(Float.round(seconds_with_ms, 2), decimals: 2) <> "s"]
      end

    Enum.join(parts, " ")
  end
end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
  @moduledoc false
  use TuistWeb, :live_view
  use Noora

  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge

  alias Noora.Filter
  alias Tuist.Gradle
  alias Tuist.Gradle.LiveTraces
  alias Tuist.Gradle.Waterfall
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.Tests
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query

  @table_page_size 25

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns

    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
      {:error, :not_found} ->
        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")

      {:ok, build} ->
        if build.project_id != project.id do
          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
        end

        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    end
  end

  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)

    test_run =
      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
        {:ok, test} -> test
        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
      end

    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)

    download_throughput =
      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    upload_throughput =
      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")

    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0

    if Phoenix.LiveView.connected?(socket) do
      Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(Tuist.PubSub, "gradle_traces:#{project.id}:#{build.id}")
    end

    socket
    |> assign(:build, build)
    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)
    |> assign(:cache_hit_rate, Gradle.cache_hit_rate(build))
    |> assign(:cache_download_bytes, aggregates.cache_download_bytes)
    |> assign(:cache_upload_bytes, aggregates.cache_upload_bytes)
    |> assign(:download_throughput, download_throughput)
    |> assign(:upload_throughput, upload_throughput)
    |> assign(:title, title)
    |> assign(:head_title, "#{title} · #{slug} · Tuist")
    |> assign(:live_spans, [])
    |> assign(:waterfall_data, nil)
  end

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build tab data to the socket during handle_params.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_handle_params(socket, params) do
    selected_tab = params["tab"] || "overview"
    uri = URI.new!("?" <> URI.encode_query(params))

    socket
    |> assign(:selected_tab, selected_tab)
    |> assign(:uri, uri)
    |> assign_tab_data(selected_tab, params)
  end

  defp build_run_path(socket) do
    %{selected_account: account, selected_project: project, build: build} = socket.assigns
    "/#{account.name}/#{project.name}/builds/build-runs/#{build.id}"
  end

  def handle_event("search-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("search-cacheable-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("add_filter", %{"value" => filter_id}, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_params =
      filter_id
      |> Filter.Operations.add_filter_to_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_params)}")
     |> push_event("open-dropdown", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-dropdown"})
     |> push_event("open-popover", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-popover"})}
  end

  def handle_event("update_filter", params, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_query_params =
      params
      |> Filter.Operations.update_filters_in_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_query_params)}")
     |> push_event("close-dropdown", %{id: "all", all: true})
     |> push_event("close-popover", %{id: "all", all: true})}
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "overview", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters = Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_task_filters())
    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_sort_by(params["tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["tasks-sort-order"] || "asc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:tasks, tasks)
    |> assign(:tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:tasks_filter, params["tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_by, params["tasks-sort-by"] || "started_at")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "gradle-cache", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters =
      Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_cacheable_task_filters())

    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["cacheable-tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["cacheable-tasks-sort-order"] || "desc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: [%{field: :cacheable, op: :==, value: true}] ++ text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["cacheable-tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {cacheable_tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks, cacheable_tasks)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_filter, params["cacheable-tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_by, params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"] || "duration_ms")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_cacheable_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "live-traces", _params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id
    spans = LiveTraces.get_spans(build_id)

    socket
    |> assign(:live_spans, spans)
    |> assign(:waterfall_data, Waterfall.build(spans))
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, _tab, _params), do: socket

  defp define_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Outcome"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :up_to_date, :executed, :failed, :skipped, :no_source],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit"),
          up_to_date: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed"),
          failed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed"),
          skipped: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped"),
          no_source: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_text_flop_filters(nil), do: []
  defp build_text_flop_filters(""), do: []

  defp build_text_flop_filters(filter_text) do
    [%{field: :task_path, op: :like, value: filter_text}]
  end

  defp build_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(value) when value in ["task_path", "duration_ms", "started_at"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(_), do: :started_at

  defp define_cacheable_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Status"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :executed],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Missed")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(value) when value in ["duration_ms", "cache_artifact_size"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(_), do: :duration_ms

  def sort_icon("desc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_down"
  def sort_icon("asc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_up"

  def sort_order_patch_value(category, current_category, current_order) do
    if category == current_category do
      if current_order == "asc", do: "desc", else: "asc"
    else
      "asc"
    end
  end

  defp outcome_color("local_hit"), do: "success"
  defp outcome_color("remote_hit"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("up_to_date"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("executed"), do: "secondary"
  defp outcome_color("failed"), do: "destructive"
  defp outcome_color(_), do: "secondary"

  defp outcome_label("local_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit")
  defp outcome_label("remote_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit")
  defp outcome_label("up_to_date"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date")
  defp outcome_label("executed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed")
  defp outcome_label("failed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed")
  defp outcome_label("skipped"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped")
  defp outcome_label("no_source"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
  defp outcome_label(other), do: other

  defp format_duration(duration_ms) do
    DateFormatter.format_duration_from_milliseconds(duration_ms)
  end

  defp started_after(task, build_started_at) do
    if task.started_at && build_started_at do
      diff_ms = NaiveDateTime.diff(task.started_at, build_started_at, :millisecond)
      format_started_after_ms(max(diff_ms, 0))
    else
      dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "—")
    end
  end

  defp format_started_after_ms(0), do: "0.00s"
  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) when ms < 1000, do: "#{ms}ms"

  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) do
    hours = div(ms, 3_600_000)
    remainder = rem(ms, 3_600_000)
    minutes = div(remainder, 60_000)
    remainder = rem(remainder, 60_000)
    seconds = div(remainder, 1_000)
    millis = rem(remainder, 1_000)

    parts = []
    parts = if hours > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{hours}h"], else: parts
    parts = if minutes > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{minutes}m"], else: parts

    parts =
      if ms > 60_000 and seconds > 0 do
        parts ++ ["#{seconds}s"]
      else
        seconds_with_ms = seconds + millis / 1000
        parts ++ [:erlang.float_to_binary(Float.round(seconds_with_ms, 2), decimals: 2) <> "s"]
      end

    Enum.join(parts, " ")
  end
end

available_filters}} = socket, params) do cas_outputs_search = params["cas-outputs-search"] || "" cas_outputs_sort_by = params["cas-outputs-sort-by"] || "compressed-size" default_sort_order = case cas_outputs_sort_by do "node-id" -> "asc" _ -> "desc" end cas_outputs_sort_order = params["cas-outputs-sort-order"] || default_sort_order cas_outputs_page = params["cas-outputs-page"] |> to_string() |> Integer.parse() |> case do {int, _} -> int :error -> 1 end flop_filters = cas_outputs_filters(run, params, available_filters, cas_outputs_search) order_by = cas_outputs_order_by(cas_outputs_sort_by) order_directions = map_sort_order(cas_outputs_sort_order) options = %{ filters: flop_filters, page: cas_outputs_page, page_size: 50, order_by: order_by, assign(:cas_outputs_search, cas_outputs_search) |> assign(:cas_outputs, outputs) |> assign(:cas_outputs_page, cas_outputs_page) |> assign(:cas_outputs_meta, outputs_meta) |> assign(:cas_outputs_active_filters, filters) |> assign(:cas_outputs_sort_by, cas_outputs_sort_by) |> assign(:cas_outputs_sort_order, cas_outputs_sort_order) end def cas_outputs_column_patch_sort( %{uri: uri, cas_outputs_sort_by: cas_outputs_sort_by, cas_outputs_sort_order: cas_outputs_sort_order} = _assigns, column_value ) do sort_order = case {cas_outputs_sort_by == column_value, cas_outputs_sort_order} do {true, "asc"} -> "desc" {true, _} -> "asc" {false, _} -> "asc" end "?#{uri.query |> Query.put("cas-outputs-sort-by", column_value) |> Query.put("cas-outputs-sort-order", sort_order) |> Query.drop("cas-outputs-page")}" end def cas_outputs_dropdown_item_patch_sort(cas_outputs_sort_by, uri) do query = uri.query |> Query.put("cas-outputs-sort-by", cas_outputs_sort_by) |> Query.drop("cas-outputs-page") |> Query.drop("cas-outputs-sort-order") "?#{query}" end # credo:disable-for-next-line Credo.Check.Refactor.CyclomaticComplexity def cache_chart_border_radius(local_hits, remote_hits, misses, category) do has_local = local_hits > 0 has_remote = remote_hits > 0 has_misses = misses > 0 case category do :local when has_local -> if not has_remote and not has_misses do [6, 6, 6, 6] else [6, 0, 0, 6] end :remote when has_remote -> cond do not has_local and not has_misses -> [6, 6, 6, 6] not has_local and not has_misses -> [6, 6, 6, 6] not has_local and has_misses -> [6, 0, 0, 6] has_local and not has_misses -> [0, 6, 6, 0] true -> [0, 0, 0, 0] end :misses when has_misses -> if not has_local and not has_remote do [6, 6, 6, 6] else [0, 6, 6, 0] end _ -> [0, 0, 0, 0] end end defp url?(value) when is_binary(value) do case URI.parse(value) do %URI{scheme: scheme} when scheme in ["http", "https"] -> true _ -> false end end defp url?(_), do: false end

<.card title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Cache Summary")} icon="chart_arcs" data-part="cache-summary-card" > <.card_section data-part="widgets-card-section"> <.widget title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Task hits")} description={ dgettext( "dashboard_gradle", "Cacheable tasks that were retrieved from cache (local + remote)." ) } value={@from_cache} id="widget-task-hits" /> <.widget title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Task misses")} description={ dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Cacheable tasks that were not found in cache.") } value={@cache_misses} id="widget-task-misses" /> <.widget title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Hit rate")} description={ dgettext( "dashboard_gradle", "The percentage of cacheable tasks that were cache hits (local + remote)." ) } value={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "%{hit_rate}%", hit_rate: @cache_hit_rate)} id="widget-hit-rate" empty={@cacheable_count == 0} /> <.widget title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Cache downloads")} description={ dgettext( "dashboard_gradle", "Total size of cache artifacts downloaded from remote cache." ) } value={ByteFormatter.format_bytes(@cache_download_bytes)} id="widget-cache-downloads" /> <.widget title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Cache uploads")} description={ dgettext( "dashboard_gradle", "Total size of cache artifacts uploaded to remote cache." ) } value={ByteFormatter.format_bytes(@cache_upload_bytes)} id="widget-cache-uploads" /> <.card title={dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Cacheable Tasks")} icon="database" data-part="cacheable-tasks-card" > <.card_section data-part="cacheable-tasks-outer-section"> <.card_section :if={(@build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0) > 0} data-part="cacheable-tasks-card-section" >

from_cache [ %{ name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{ focus: "series" }, data: [local_hit], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-local", itemStyle: %{ borderRadius: if(local_hit > 0 and remote_hit == 0 and misses == 0, do: [8, 8, 8, 8], else: if(local_hit > 0, do: [8, 0, 0, 8], else: [0, 0, 0, 0]) ) } }, %{ name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{ focus: "series" }, data: [remote_hit], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-remote", itemStyle: %{ borderRadius: if(remote_hit > 0 and misses == 0 and local_hit == 0, do: [8, 8, 8, 8], else: if(remote_hit > 0 and misses == 0, do: [0, 8, 8, 0], else: [0, 0, 0, 0] ) ) } }, %{ name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Missed"), type: "bar", stack: "total", emphasis: %{ focus: "series" }, data: [misses], color: "var:hits-chart-legend-missed", itemStyle: %{ borderRadius: if(misses > 0 and from_cache == 0, do: [8, 8, 8, 8], else: if(misses > 0, do: [0, 8, 8, 0], else: [0, 0, 0, 0]) ) } } ] } x_axis_min={0} x_axis_max={@build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0} />

content.content: The file /Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/gradle/otlp_parser.ex has been updated. All occurrences of 'get_in(' were successfully replaced with 'nested_get('.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule Tuist.Gradle.OTLPParser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse_protobuf(binary) when is_binary(binary) do
    request = :opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb.decode_msg(binary, :export_trace_service_request)
    extract_spans(request)
  end

  def parse_json(body) when is_map(body) do
    resource_spans = Map.get(body, "resourceSpans", [])

    Enum.flat_map(resource_spans, fn rs ->
      resource_attributes = extract_json_attributes(get_in(rs, ["resource", "attributes"]) || [])

      scope_spans = Map.get(rs, "scopeSpans", [])

      Enum.flat_map(scope_spans, fn ss ->
        spans = Map.get(ss, "spans", [])

        Enum.map(spans, fn span ->
          %{
            trace_id: Map.get(span, "traceId", ""),
            span_id: Map.get(span, "spanId", ""),
            parent_span_id: Map.get(span, "parentSpanId", ""),
            name: Map.get(span, "name", ""),
            start_time_unix_nano: parse_nano_string(Map.get(span, "startTimeUnixNano", "0")),
            end_time_unix_nano: parse_nano_string(Map.get(span, "endTimeUnixNano", "0")),
            status: extract_json_status(Map.get(span, "status", %{})),
            attributes: extract_json_attributes(Map.get(span, "attributes", [])),
            resource_attributes: resource_attributes
          }
        end)
      end)
    end)
  end

  defp extract_spans(request) do
    resource_spans = elem(request, 1)

    Enum.flat_map(resource_spans, fn rs ->
      resource = elem(rs, 1)
      resource_attributes = extract_pb_attributes(elem(resource, 1))

      scope_spans = elem(rs, 2)

      Enum.flat_map(scope_spans, fn ss ->
        spans = elem(ss, 2)

        Enum.map(spans, fn span ->
          %{
            trace_id: encode_hex(elem(span, 1)),
            span_id: encode_hex(elem(span, 2)),
            parent_span_id: encode_hex(elem(span, 4)),
            name: to_string(elem(span, 5)),
            start_time_unix_nano: elem(span, 7),
            end_time_unix_nano: elem(span, 8),
            status: extract_pb_status(elem(span, 14)),
            attributes: extract_pb_attributes(elem(span, 9)),
            resource_attributes: resource_attributes
          }
        end)
      end)
    end)
  end

  defp extract_pb_attributes(attrs) when is_list(attrs) do
    Map.new(attrs, fn kv ->
      key = to_string(elem(kv, 1))
      value = extract_pb_value(elem(kv, 2))
      {key, value}
    end)
  end

  defp extract_pb_attributes(_), do: %{}

  defp extract_pb_value(any_value) when is_tuple(any_value) do
    case elem(any_value, 0) do
      :any_value ->
        cond do
          elem(any_value, 1) != "" and is_binary(elem(any_value, 1)) ->
            elem(any_value, 1)

          elem(any_value, 3) != 0 ->
            elem(any_value, 3)

          elem(any_value, 4) != 0.0 ->
            elem(any_value, 4)

          elem(any_value, 2) != false ->
            elem(any_value, 2)

          true ->
            to_string(elem(any_value, 1))
        end

      _ ->
        ""
    end
  end

  defp extract_pb_value(_), do: ""

  defp extract_pb_status(status) when is_tuple(status) do
    case elem(status, 0) do
      :status ->
        code = elem(status, 2)

        case code do
          :STATUS_CODE_OK -> "ok"
          :STATUS_CODE_ERROR -> "error"
          _ -> "unset"
        end

      _ ->
        "unset"
    end
  end

  defp extract_pb_status(_), do: "unset"

  defp extract_json_status(%{"code" => code}) do
    case code do
      1 -> "ok"
      2 -> "error"
      _ -> "unset"
    end
  end

  defp extract_json_status(_), do: "unset"

  defp extract_json_attributes(attrs) when is_list(attrs) do
    Map.new(attrs, fn attr ->
      key = Map.get(attr, "key", "")
      value = extract_json_value(Map.get(attr, "value", %{}))
      {key, value}
    end)
  end

  defp extract_json_attributes(_), do: %{}

  defp extract_json_value(%{"stringValue" => v}), do: v
  defp extract_json_value(%{"intValue" => v}) when is_binary(v), do: String.to_integer(v)
  defp extract_json_value(%{"intValue" => v}), do: v
  defp extract_json_value(%{"doubleValue" => v}), do: v
  defp extract_json_value(%{"boolValue" => v}), do: v
  defp extract_json_value(_), do: ""

  defp encode_hex(bytes) when is_binary(bytes) and byte_size(bytes) > 0 do
    Base.encode16(bytes, case: :lower)
  end

  defp encode_hex(_), do: ""

  defp parse_nano_string(v) when is_binary(v) do
    case Integer.parse(v) do
      {n, _} -> n
      :error -> 0
    end
  end

  defp parse_nano_string(v) when is_integer(v), do: v
  defp parse_nano_string(_), do: 0

  defp get_in(map, []), do: map

  defp get_in(map, [key | rest]) when is_map(map) do
    get_in(Map.get(map, key), rest)
  end

  defp get_in(_, _), do: nil
end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule TuistWeb.GradleBuildLive do
  @moduledoc false
  use TuistWeb, :live_view
  use Noora

  import TuistWeb.Runs.RanByBadge

  alias Noora.Filter
  alias Tuist.Gradle
  alias Tuist.Gradle.LiveTraces
  alias Tuist.Gradle.Waterfall
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.Tests
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ByteFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.DateFormatter
  alias Tuist.Utilities.ThroughputFormatter
  alias TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError
  alias TuistWeb.Utilities.Query

  @table_page_size 25

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build data to the socket during mount.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_mount(socket, build_id) do
    %{selected_project: project, selected_account: account} = socket.assigns

    case Gradle.get_build(build_id) do
      {:error, :not_found} ->
        raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")

      {:ok, build} ->
        if build.project_id != project.id do
          raise NotFoundError, dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Build not found.")
        end

        assign_build(socket, build, account)
    end
  end

  defp assign_build(socket, build, account) do
    build = Repo.preload(build, :built_by_account)

    test_run =
      case Tests.get_latest_test_by_gradle_build_id(build.id) do
        {:ok, test} -> test
        {:error, :not_found} -> nil
      end

    build_started_at = Gradle.build_started_at(build.id)
    aggregates = Gradle.task_cache_aggregates(build.id)

    download_throughput =
      if aggregates.download_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_download_bytes / (aggregates.download_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    upload_throughput =
      if aggregates.upload_duration_ms > 0,
        do: aggregates.cache_upload_bytes / (aggregates.upload_duration_ms / 1000),
        else: 0

    slug = "#{account.name}/#{socket.assigns.selected_project.name}"
    title = build.root_project_name || dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Gradle Build")

    local_hits = build.tasks_local_hit_count || 0
    remote_hits = build.tasks_remote_hit_count || 0
    from_cache = local_hits + remote_hits
    cacheable = build.cacheable_tasks_count || 0

    if Phoenix.LiveView.connected?(socket) do
      Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(Tuist.PubSub, "gradle_traces:#{project.id}:#{build.id}")
    end

    socket
    |> assign(:build, build)
    |> assign(:test_run, test_run)
    |> assign(:build_started_at, build_started_at)
    |> assign(:from_cache, from_cache)
    |> assign(:cache_misses, cacheable - from_cache)
    |> assign(:cacheable_count, cacheable)
    |> assign(:cache_hit_rate, Gradle.cache_hit_rate(build))
    |> assign(:cache_download_bytes, aggregates.cache_download_bytes)
    |> assign(:cache_upload_bytes, aggregates.cache_upload_bytes)
    |> assign(:download_throughput, download_throughput)
    |> assign(:upload_throughput, upload_throughput)
    |> assign(:title, title)
    |> assign(:head_title, "#{title} · #{slug} · Tuist")
    |> assign(:live_spans, [])
    |> assign(:waterfall_data, nil)
  end

  @doc """
  Assigns gradle build tab data to the socket during handle_params.
  Called from BuildRunLive when the project is a gradle project.
  """
  def assign_handle_params(socket, params) do
    selected_tab = params["tab"] || "overview"
    uri = URI.new!("?" <> URI.encode_query(params))

    socket
    |> assign(:selected_tab, selected_tab)
    |> assign(:uri, uri)
    |> assign_tab_data(selected_tab, params)
  end

  defp build_run_path(socket) do
    %{selected_account: account, selected_project: project, build: build} = socket.assigns
    "/#{account.name}/#{project.name}/builds/build-runs/#{build.id}"
  end

  def handle_info({:gradle_trace_spans, _build_id, new_spans}, socket) do
    all_spans = socket.assigns.live_spans ++ new_spans

    if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "live-traces" do
      {:noreply,
       socket
       |> assign(:live_spans, all_spans)
       |> assign(:waterfall_data, Waterfall.build(all_spans))}
    else
      {:noreply, assign(socket, :live_spans, all_spans)}
    end
  end

  def handle_event("search-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("search-cacheable-tasks", %{"search" => search}, socket) do
    query =
      socket.assigns.uri.query
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-filter", search)
      |> Query.put("cacheable-tasks-page", "1")

    {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{query}")}
  end

  def handle_event("add_filter", %{"value" => filter_id}, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_params =
      filter_id
      |> Filter.Operations.add_filter_to_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_params)}")
     |> push_event("open-dropdown", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-dropdown"})
     |> push_event("open-popover", %{id: "filter-#{filter_id}-value-popover"})}
  end

  def handle_event("update_filter", params, socket) do
    page_param =
      if socket.assigns.selected_tab == "gradle-cache",
        do: "cacheable-tasks-page",
        else: "tasks-page"

    updated_query_params =
      params
      |> Filter.Operations.update_filters_in_query(socket)
      |> Map.put(page_param, "1")

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> push_patch(to: "#{build_run_path(socket)}?#{URI.encode_query(updated_query_params)}")
     |> push_event("close-dropdown", %{id: "all", all: true})
     |> push_event("close-popover", %{id: "all", all: true})}
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "overview", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters = Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_task_filters())
    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_sort_by(params["tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["tasks-sort-order"] || "asc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:tasks, tasks)
    |> assign(:tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:tasks_filter, params["tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_by, params["tasks-sort-by"] || "started_at")
    |> assign(:tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "gradle-cache", params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id

    filters =
      Filter.Operations.decode_filters_from_query(params, define_cacheable_task_filters())

    text_filters = build_text_flop_filters(params["cacheable-tasks-filter"])
    dropdown_filters = build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters)
    sort_by = ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"])
    sort_order = params["cacheable-tasks-sort-order"] || "desc"

    flop_params = %{
      filters: [%{field: :cacheable, op: :==, value: true}] ++ text_filters ++ dropdown_filters,
      page: String.to_integer(params["cacheable-tasks-page"] || "1"),
      page_size: @table_page_size,
      order_by: [sort_by],
      order_directions: [String.to_atom(sort_order)]
    }

    {cacheable_tasks, meta} = Gradle.list_tasks(build_id, flop_params)

    socket
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks, cacheable_tasks)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page, meta.current_page)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_page_count, meta.total_pages)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_filter, params["cacheable-tasks-filter"] || "")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_by, params["cacheable-tasks-sort-by"] || "duration_ms")
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_sort_order, sort_order)
    |> assign(:cacheable_tasks_active_filters, filters)
    |> assign(:available_filters, define_cacheable_task_filters())
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, "live-traces", _params) do
    build_id = socket.assigns.build.id
    spans = LiveTraces.get_spans(build_id)

    socket
    |> assign(:live_spans, spans)
    |> assign(:waterfall_data, Waterfall.build(spans))
  end

  defp assign_tab_data(socket, _tab, _params), do: socket

  defp define_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Outcome"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :up_to_date, :executed, :failed, :skipped, :no_source],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit"),
          up_to_date: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed"),
          failed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed"),
          skipped: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped"),
          no_source: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_text_flop_filters(nil), do: []
  defp build_text_flop_filters(""), do: []

  defp build_text_flop_filters(filter_text) do
    [%{field: :task_path, op: :like, value: filter_text}]
  end

  defp build_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(value) when value in ["task_path", "duration_ms", "started_at"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_sort_by(_), do: :started_at

  defp define_cacheable_task_filters do
    [
      %Filter.Filter{
        id: "outcome",
        field: :outcome,
        display_name: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Status"),
        type: :option,
        options: [:local_hit, :remote_hit, :executed],
        options_display_names: %{
          local_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local"),
          remote_hit: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote"),
          executed: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Missed")
        },
        operator: :==,
        value: nil
      }
    ]
  end

  defp build_cacheable_task_flop_filters(filters) do
    filters
    |> Enum.map(fn filter ->
      "outcome" = filter.id
      %{filter | value: if(filter.value, do: Atom.to_string(filter.value))}
    end)
    |> Filter.Operations.convert_filters_to_flop()
  end

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(value) when value in ["duration_ms", "cache_artifact_size"],
    do: String.to_existing_atom(value)

  defp ensure_allowed_cacheable_sort_by(_), do: :duration_ms

  def sort_icon("desc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_down"
  def sort_icon("asc"), do: "square_rounded_arrow_up"

  def sort_order_patch_value(category, current_category, current_order) do
    if category == current_category do
      if current_order == "asc", do: "desc", else: "asc"
    else
      "asc"
    end
  end

  defp outcome_color("local_hit"), do: "success"
  defp outcome_color("remote_hit"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("up_to_date"), do: "information"
  defp outcome_color("executed"), do: "secondary"
  defp outcome_color("failed"), do: "destructive"
  defp outcome_color(_), do: "secondary"

  defp outcome_label("local_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Local hit")
  defp outcome_label("remote_hit"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Remote hit")
  defp outcome_label("up_to_date"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Up-to-date")
  defp outcome_label("executed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Executed")
  defp outcome_label("failed"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Failed")
  defp outcome_label("skipped"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "Skipped")
  defp outcome_label("no_source"), do: dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "No source")
  defp outcome_label(other), do: other

  defp format_duration(duration_ms) do
    DateFormatter.format_duration_from_milliseconds(duration_ms)
  end

  defp started_after(task, build_started_at) do
    if task.started_at && build_started_at do
      diff_ms = NaiveDateTime.diff(task.started_at, build_started_at, :millisecond)
      format_started_after_ms(max(diff_ms, 0))
    else
      dgettext("dashboard_gradle", "—")
    end
  end

  defp format_started_after_ms(0), do: "0.00s"
  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) when ms < 1000, do: "#{ms}ms"

  defp format_started_after_ms(ms) do
    hours = div(ms, 3_600_000)
    remainder = rem(ms, 3_600_000)
    minutes = div(remainder, 60_000)
    remainder = rem(remainder, 60_000)
    seconds = div(remainder, 1_000)
    millis = rem(remainder, 1_000)

    parts = []
    parts = if hours > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{hours}h"], else: parts
    parts = if minutes > 0, do: parts ++ ["#{minutes}m"], else: parts

    parts =
      if ms > 60_000 and seconds > 0 do
        parts ++ ["#{seconds}s"]
      else
        seconds_with_ms = seconds + millis / 1000
        parts ++ [:erlang.float_to_binary(Float.round(seconds_with_ms, 2), decimals: 2) <> "s"]
      end

    Enum.join(parts, " ")
  end
end

file.content:
defmodule Tuist do
  @moduledoc """
  Tuist keeps the contexts that define your domain
  and business logic.

  Contexts are also responsible for managing your data, regardless
  if it comes from the database, an external API or others.
  """
  use Boundary,
    deps: [],
    exports: [
      # Marketing
      # -----
      # These modules contain utilities that are used for the marketing-related
      # routes and features.
      Marketing.Blog,
      Marketing.Content,
      Marketing.Pages,
      Marketing.Changelog,
      Marketing.OpenGraph,
      Marketing.Newsletter,
      Marketing.BlogContentProcessor,
      Marketing.Customers,
      # App
      # -----
      # This module contains Tuist features that are not expected to have inter-dependencies
      # among them. They must only depend on core and utility modules.
      Bundles,
      Bundles.Bundle,
      Cache,
      Cache.Analytics,
      CacheEndpoints,
      Gradle,
      Gradle.Analytics,
      Gradle.Build,
      Gradle.Task,
      Gradle.CacheEvent,
      CacheActionItems,
      CommandEvents,
      CommandEvents.Event,
      Registry.Swift.Packages,
      Registry.Swift.Packages.Package,
      Registry.Swift.Packages.PackageManifest,
      Registry.Swift.Packages.PackageRelease,
      Builds,
      Builds.Build,
      Builds.CASOutput,
      Builds.Analytics,
      Runs.Analytics,
      Tests,
      Tests.Test,
      Tests.TestCase,
      Tests.Analytics,
      # App
      # -----
      # They are modules that are core to the Tuist domain (e.g. accounts) and that other
      # features build upon.
      API.Pipeline,
      Accounts,
      Accounts.Account,
      Accounts.AccountCacheEndpoint,
      Accounts.Organization,
      Accounts.AuthenticatedAccount,
      Accounts.AccountToken,
      Accounts.User,
      Authentication,
      Authorization,
      Guardian,
      OIDC,
      Authorization.Checks,
      Billing,
      Billing.Subscription,
      AppBuilds,
      AppBuilds.Preview,
      AppBuilds.AppBuild,
      Projects,
      Projects.Project,
      Projects.Workers.CleanProjectWorker,
      QA,
      QA.Run,
      QA.Step,
      QA.Screenshot,
      QA.Log,
      QA.Logs.Buffer,
      QA.Workers.TestWorker,
      Apple,
      Xcode,
      Xcode.XcodeGraph,
      Xcode.XcodeProject,
      Xcode.XcodeTarget,
      Loops,
      Namespace,
      Namespace.JWTToken,
      QA,
      QA.LaunchArgumentGroup,
      VCS.GitHubAppInstallation,
      Alerts,
      Alerts.Alert,
      Alerts.AlertRule,
      Alerts.Workers.AlertWorker,
      Slack,
      Slack.Client,
      Slack.Installation,
      Slack.Reports,
      Slack.Workers.ReportWorker,
      # Support
      # -----
      # These modules represent Tuist-agnostic utilities that are used by other features.
      # As of today, some of these utilities still have knowledge of the Tuist domain,
      # but we should aim to make them domain-agnostic. To know if they are Tuist-agnostic
      # a good rule of thumb is to ask if they can work as a standalone library.
      Analytics,
      Environment,
      Ecto.Utils,
      GitHub.Releases,
      Incidents,
      License,
      PubSub,
      KeyValueStore,
      ClickHouseRepo,
      ClickHouseFlop,
      Markdown,
      # We should not be exposing this one
      Repo,
      Storage,
      Tasks,
      Time,
      Utilities.ByteFormatter,
      Utilities.DateFormatter,
      Utilities.ThroughputFormatter,
      VCS,
      UUIDv7,
      OAuth.Apple,
      OAuth.Okta
    ]
end

Links: [{"title":"Gradle - Plugin: com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build","url":"https://plugins.gradle.org/plugin/com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build"},{"title":"Getting started | OpenTelemetry","url":"https://opentelemetry.io/docs/zero-code/java/spring-boot-starter/getting-started/"},{"title":"Gradle - Plugins","url":"https://plugins.gradle.org/search?term=opentelemetry"},{"title":"opentelemetry-java-instrumentation/examples/extension/build.gradle at main · open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java-instrumentation","url":"https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java-instrumentation/blob/main/examples/extension/build.gradle"},{"title":"Maven Repository: io.opentelemetry","url":"https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/io.opentelemetry"},{"title":"GitHub - craigatk/opentelemetry-gradle-plugin: Instrument your Gradle build with OpenTelemetry observability to find and eliminate bottlenecks in your build","url":"https://github.com/craigatk/opentelemetry-gradle-plugin"},{"title":"Find and Fix Bottlenecks in Your Gradle Builds With OpenTelemetry and Honeycomb","url":"https://www.honeycomb.io/blog/gradle-builds-opentelemery-honeycomb"},{"title":"Maven Repository: io.opentelemetry » opentelemetry-api","url":"https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/io.opentelemetry/opentelemetry-api"},{"title":"opentelemetry-java-contrib/maven-extension/README.md at main · open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java-contrib","url":"https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java-contrib/blob/main/maven-extension/README.md"},{"title":"Maven Repository: io.opentelemetry.instrumentation » gradle-plugins","url":"https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/io.opentelemetry.instrumentation/gradle-plugins"}]

Based on the search results, I found the maven coordinates for the com.atkinsondev opentelemetry-build gradle plugin:

The Maven coordinates for adding this plugin as a dependency are: `com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build:com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build.gradle.plugin:4.6.2`

This can be used in two ways:

1. **In precompiled script plugins:**
   Add to `<convention-plugins-build>/build.gradle(.kts)`: `implementation("com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build:com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build.gradle.plugin:4.6.2")`

2. **Using the legacy buildscript method:**
   Add to buildscript: `classpath("com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build:com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build.gradle.plugin:4.6.2")`


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

This can be used in two ways:

content.text: The artifact coordinates for Gradle plugins on the Portal use a different format. Since this is `compileOnly` (we're just using it for referencing the extension class via reflection), I should actually use reflection entirely and not need the dependency at all. The current code already uses reflection (`extensionClass.getMethod(...)`) so the compileOnly dependency isn't actually needed. Let me remove it and make the approach purely reflection-based.

content.content: The file /Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/gradle/waterfall.ex has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.oldString: def build(spans) when is_list(spans) and length(spans) == 0, do: nil
toolUseResult.originalFile:
defmodule Tuist.Gradle.Waterfall do
  @moduledoc false

  def build(spans) when is_list(spans) and length(spans) == 0, do: nil

  def build(spans) do
    root_start = find_root_start(spans)
    now_ns = System.os_time(:nanosecond)

    sorted_spans = Enum.sort_by(spans, & &1.start_time_unix_nano)

    categories = Enum.map(sorted_spans, & &1.name)

    spacer_data =
      Enum.map(sorted_spans, fn span ->
        offset_ms = div(span.start_time_unix_nano - root_start, 1_000_000)
        max(offset_ms, 0)
      end)

    duration_data =
      Enum.map(sorted_spans, fn span ->
        end_time =
          if span.end_time_unix_nano == 0,
            do: now_ns,
            else: span.end_time_unix_nano

        duration_ms = div(end_time - span.start_time_unix_nano, 1_000_000)
        max(duration_ms, 1)
      end)

    colors =
      Enum.map(sorted_spans, fn span ->
        cond do
          span.end_time_unix_nano == 0 -> "var:noora-chart-primary"
          span.status == "error" -> "var:noora-chart-destructive"
          true -> "var:noora-chart-secondary"
        end
      end)

    max_time_ms =
      spacer_data
      |> Enum.zip(duration_data)
      |> Enum.map(fn {spacer, dur} -> spacer + dur end)
      |> Enum.max(fn -> 0 end)

    %{
      categories: categories,
      series: [
        %{
          name: "offset",
          type: "bar",
          stack: "total",
          data: spacer_data,
          itemStyle: %{color: "transparent"},
          emphasis: %{itemStyle: %{color: "transparent"}},
          tooltip: %{show: false}
        },
        %{
          name: "duration",
          type: "bar",
          stack: "total",
          data:
            [duration_data, colors]
            |> Enum.zip()
            |> Enum.map(fn {value, color} ->
              %{value: value, itemStyle: %{color: color, borderRadius: [0, 4, 4, 0]}}
            end)
        }
      ],
      max_time_ms: max_time_ms
    }
  end

  defp find_root_start(spans) do
    spans
    |> Enum.filter(fn span -> span.parent_span_id == "" or is_nil(span.parent_span_id) end)
    |> case do
      [root | _] -> root.start_time_unix_nano
      [] -> spans |> Enum.min_by(& &1.start_time_unix_nano) |> Map.get(:start_time_unix_nano)
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Everything is implemented and compiles. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

### New Files (Server - Elixir)

1. **`server/lib/tuist/gradle/otlp_parser.ex`** - Parses OTLP trace data from both protobuf (using the existing `:opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb` decoder) and JSON formats. Extracts spans into normalized maps with trace_id, span_id, name, timestamps, status, and attributes.

2. **`server/lib/tuist/gradle/live_traces.ex`** - ETS-backed GenServer for in-memory span storage per build. Broadcasts new spans via PubSub to `"gradle_traces:#{project_id}:#{build_id}"`. Auto-cleans entries older than 30 minutes every 5 minutes. Caps at 10,000 spans per build.

3. **`server/lib/tuist/gradle/waterfall.ex`** - Converts flat span lists into ECharts horizontal stacked bar chart data (Gantt-style). Uses transparent spacers for offsets and colored duration bars. Color-codes: blue (running), green (success), red (error).

4. **`server/lib/tuist_web/controllers/api/gradle_traces_controller.ex`** - OTLP HTTP receiver endpoint. Accepts `application/x-protobuf` and `application/json`. Uses same auth pipeline as existing Gradle controller (LoaderPlug + AuthorizationPlug). Extracts `tuist.build.id` from resource attributes.

### Modified Files (Server)

5. **`server/lib/tuist_web/router.ex`** - Added `post "/traces/v1/traces"` route inside the `/gradle` scope.

6. **`server/lib/tuist/application.ex`** - Added `Tuist.Gradle.LiveTraces` to the supervisor children.

7. **`server/lib/tuist.ex`** - Added `Gradle.LiveTraces`, `Gradle.OTLPParser`, and `Gradle.Waterfall` to boundary exports.

8. **`server/lib/tuist_web/live/gradle_build_live.ex`** - Added PubSub subscription on mount, `handle_info` for incoming trace spans, and `"live-traces"` tab data loading from LiveTraces + Waterfall builder.

9. **`server/lib/tuist_web/live/gradle_build_live.html.heex`** - Added "Live Traces" tab with Noora `.chart` waterfall visualization and empty state.

10. **`server/lib/tuist_web/live/build_run_live.ex`** - Added `handle_info` delegation for `{:gradle_trace_spans, _, _}` messages to GradleBuildLive.

### Modified Files (Gradle Plugin - Kotlin)

11. **`gradle/src/main/kotlin/dev/tuist/gradle/TuistBuildInsights.kt`** - Added `configureOpenTelemetryPlugin()` method to `TuistBuildInsightsPlugin`. When the `com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build` plugin is detected, auto-configures it via reflection with the correct endpoint, auth headers, service name, and `tuist.build.id` custom tag.

### Verification
- Server compiles with `--warnings-as-errors`: clean
- Credo: no issues
- Gradle plugin: BUILD SUCCESSFUL

Here's how to test this end-to-end:

## 1. Start the dev server

```bash
cd server && mise run dev
```

## 2. Send a test OTLP payload with curl

Pick an existing Gradle build ID from your dev database, or use a fake one. You need a valid project token for auth.

**JSON format (easiest to craft by hand):**

```bash
BUILD_ID="test-build-$(uuidgen)"

curl -X POST "http://localhost:REDACTED" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "resourceSpans": [{
      "resource": {
        "attributes": [
          {"key": "tuist.build.id", "value": {"stringValue": "'$BUILD_ID'"}},
          {"key": "service.name", "value": {"stringValue": "gradle-test"}}
        ]
      },
      "scopeSpans": [{
        "spans": [
          {
            "traceId": "aaaabbbbccccddddeeee111122223333",
            "spanId": "1111111111111111",
            "parentSpanId": "",
            "name": "root-build",
            "startTimeUnixNano": "1700000000000000000",
            "endTimeUnixNano": "1700000030000000000",
            "status": {"code": 1},
            "attributes": []
          },
          {
            "traceId": "aaaabbbbccccddddeeee111122223333",
            "spanId": "2222222222222222",
            "parentSpanId": "1111111111111111",
            "name": ":app:compileKotlin",
            "startTimeUnixNano": "1700000001000000000",
            "endTimeUnixNano": "1700000015000000000",
            "status": {"code": 1},
            "attributes": []
          },
          {
            "traceId": "aaaabbbbccccddddeeee111122223333",
            "spanId": "3333333333333333",
            "parentSpanId": "1111111111111111",
            "name": ":app:compileJava",
            "startTimeUnixNano": "1700000010000000000",
            "endTimeUnixNano": "1700000025000000000",
            "status": {"code": 1},
            "attributes": []
          },
          {
            "traceId": "aaaabbbbccccddddeeee111122223333",
            "spanId": "4444444444444444",
            "parentSpanId": "1111111111111111",
            "name": ":lib:compileKotlin",
            "startTimeUnixNano": "1700000002000000000",
            "endTimeUnixNano": "0",
            "status": {},
            "attributes": []
          }
        ]
      }]
    }]
  }'
```

You should get `{}` back with HTTP 200. The fourth span has `endTimeUnixNano: 0` so it shows as "running" (blue).

## 3. Check the Live Traces tab

Navigate to a Gradle build run in the dashboard and click the "Live Traces" tab. The `BUILD_ID` from the curl command needs to match the build you're viewing -- so the easiest approach is:

1. Create a Gradle build via the normal API first (or use an existing one)
2. Use that build's ID as the `tuist.build.id` in the curl payload
3. Open that build's page and click "Live Traces"

Alternatively, to verify the data landed in ETS, open an IEx session:

```bash
cd server && iex -S mix phx.server
```

```elixir
# Check what's stored
:ets.tab2list(:gradle_live_traces)

# Or by specific build_id
Tuist.Gradle.LiveTraces.get_spans("test-build-YOUR-UUID")
```

## 4. Test real-time updates

Open the build page on the "Live Traces" tab in a browser, then send multiple curl requests. The chart should update in real-time without a page refresh as spans arrive via PubSub.

## 5. Test with a real Gradle build (full E2E)

In a Gradle project, add both plugins to `settings.gradle.kts`:

```kotlin
plugins {
    id("dev.tuist") version "..."
    id("com.atkinsondev.opentelemetry-build") version "4.6.2"
}

tuist {
    url = "http://localhost:4000"
    project = "your-account/your-project"
}
```

The Tuist plugin should auto-configure the OTel plugin. Run a build and watch the Live Traces tab update.

## 6. Verify protobuf format works

If you want to test the protobuf path specifically, you can generate a protobuf payload from an IEx session:

```elixir
# Encode a test request
spans = [...]  # your span records
encoded = :opentelemetry_exporter_trace_service_pb.encode_msg(request, :export_trace_service_request)
File.write!("/tmp/test_traces.pb", encoded)
```

Then curl with:
```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:4000/api/projects/ACCOUNT/PROJECT/gradle/traces/v1/traces" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-protobuf" \
  --data-binary @/tmp/test_traces.pb
```

message.content: why isn't tuist auth login in the simple android app directory authenticating with localhost:8080?

content.text: Let me investigate how `tuist auth login` determines which server to connect to.

input.description: Find tuist auth login config
input.prompt:
I need to understand how `tuist auth login` determines which server URL to connect to. Search for:

1. How the CLI resolves the server URL - search for "auth login" or "auth" command handling in `cli/` Swift sources
2. Look for any `tuist.toml` or configuration file that specifies the server URL
3. Search for "localhost:8080" or "8080" in the CLI code to understand the local dev server connection
4. Check if there's a `.tuist` directory or config file concept that a project needs

Focus on understanding the full flow: what config file does a Gradle/Android project need to have for `tuist auth login` to connect to localhost:8080 instead of tuist.dev?

1→import ArgumentParser
     2→import Foundation
     3→
     4→public struct LoginCommand: AsyncParsableCommand {
     5→    public init() {}
     6→
     7→    public static var configuration: CommandConfiguration {
     8→        CommandConfiguration(
     9→            commandName: "login",
    10→            _superCommandName: "auth",
    11→            abstract: "Log in a user"
    12→        )
    13→    }
    14→
    15→    @Option(
    16→        help: "Email to authenticate with."
    17→    )
    18→    var email: String?
    19→
    20→    @Option(
    21→        help: "Password to authenticate with."
    22→    )
    23→    var password: String?
    24→
    25→    @Option(
    26→        name: .long,
    27→        help: "The URL of the server."
    28→    )
    29→    var url: String?
    30→
    31→    public func run() async throws {
    32→        try await LoginCommandService().run(
    33→            email: email,
    34→            password: password,
    35→            serverURL: url
    36→        )
    37→    }
    38→}
    39→
